text: If you are willing to give it time.  I wish there was a 'maybe' option. If you are already into movement shooters or Apex's father, then i'd say you should definitely give it a shot. But if you want in on Apex as a newcomer to the genre, oh boy will u have a hard time... See if you can follow up: 5 maps of which 3 are playable and change every 3 months, 24 different characters to play as (as of season 18 that is), 29 guns to choose from. That covers the complete basics, then you go into team synergy, ammo economy, rotations, matchups and the 'end all be all' part, the MOVEMENT. If you are aiming to start playing solo, please don't. The game was clearly made with duos or trios with comms and synergy in mind, so just grab a bud or 2, hold them hostage, sit down and play. Id say gravitate into ranked even if you are aiming for a casual experienicee cause casual lobbies are infested with higher ranked players looking for easy pickings. Even though allegedly ranked and casual claim to have the same algorithm for matchmaking, I've noticed that in ranked things are not that bad . Although the experienicee of learning Apex can feel rewarding onicee you see what is possible in the game, the dedication and nerves of steel it requires make it a rough game to recommend. In my opinion, the best balaniceed battle royale and a really good shooter that if given the attention if requires, it will be worth it, but this comes from a dude with almost 350 hrs shared playtime (between xbox and pc) and almost 140 hrs of Titanfall 2. text: TLDR; The negatives of Apex outweigh the positives after years of poor decision making and greedy business tactics from EA and Respawn. The game is regularly unbalaniceed, bugood gamey, toxic, and predatory with micro-transactions. Your time and money is likely better spent elsewhere. At it is  core, I'd say Apex Legends is a well made FPS. The game has: [*]enjoyable gun-play [*]unrivaled movement that allows for true user expression [*]generabad game mechanics that are easy to pick up and understand [*]additional in-depth game mechanics to learn and masterI really can't emphasize how nice the gun-play and movement within the game feels in comparison to other modern FPS titles. The movement is especially alluring and can have you wanting to spend hours learning mechanics like wall-bouniceing, tap-strafing, super-gliding, etc. These in-depth mechanics are personally the only true part of the game that has kept me coming back for over 1k hours.With that being said, the game's current negatives really outweigh the positives and make the overall experienicee feel almost like a chore. Current negatives inicelude: 1. Solo-Queuing.  If you solo queue, the experienicee will often be less than enjoyable. The matchmaking is very subpar and you will regularly be paired against pre-made squads and or players of a substantially different skill-level. There is no real way to combat this as the ranked playlist has the same issue. 2. Balaniceing.  The balaniceing within the game is questionable. Apex introduces new legends and weapons on a seasonal basis (which in my opinion is generally a net positive to keep things fresh). Unfortunately, the game has never found a real balanicee between all of this. You might find yourself questioning why certain legends even exist when another character simply does their role better. Respawn has tried to address this with recent updates, which unfortunately often just creates new imbalanicees. 3. Cross-input/Cross-platform gameplay is flawed and unbalaniceed.  This usually wouldn't be a huge issue if the differenicee between the inputs/platforms wasn't so substantial. Controller has aim assist which passively improves your accuracy during gunfights. Controller aim assist values come in at 40% on PC and 60% on console. Mouse and keyboard obviously has no aim assist but instead the potential to utilize tap-strafing for movement. The community is pretty torn on the subject of what's fair between these two inputs which overall has negatively affected the competitive integrity of the game. I do need to mention a majority of PC players likely do not even utilize tap strafing as it is something you need to research and teach yourself how to do. Regardless, whatever input you decide to use, it does not change the fact that the inputs are not on a level playing field. On the topic of cross-platform, PC players are unable to disable it within the settings to avoid being placed with or against console players. Be ready to be one clipped by console players on the regular. A 60% aim assist value is absurdly high and most of the community at least agrees on that lol. 4. Server issues, bugs, and cheaters.  Sadly these run pretty rampant in this game. This is obviously expected from a majority of live service games, but Apex can go seasons without fixing bugs that arguably should have been a focus from the start. EA and Respawn also certainly have the funding to maintain their servers and security, which begins to make you question where their priorities lie. 5. Predatory Micro-transactions.  The purchasable cosmetics in Apex are ridiculously expensive. Obviously you don't have to interact with this if you don't want to, but the majority of "events" in game are almost purely cosmetics. New skins will cost around $20 a piece and heirlooms will cost you around $160 to secure them during their respective event. EA and Respawn have made it clear this isn't changing anytime soon as they have maintained this business model throughout the years and recently have even gone as far as charging full price for recolors of existing skins. 6. Toxic Community.  Obviously games like this and toxicity often come hand-in-hand so there really is no surprise there. I wouldn't say the entire community is toxic, in fact I do think there are a lot of really chill people who play this game. However it is fairly common to have teammates be unnecessarily toxic even in casuabad games. It can become especially taxing due to the games current state.it is  unfortunate to see where Apex is now, and I really have always wished for it to succeed. However Respawn and EA are doing too little too late to fix the issues with their game and show the community that they are listening. These last two seasons were supposed to be a saving grace for the game and unfortunately have just been more of the same.
label: FALSE

text: Truly a Marvel of Modern Gaming I have almost 1500 hours on this game so I think that somewhat qualifies me to write a review. Apex Legends is a game that gets so many things right, yet misses the mark entirely on the most important aspects of a multiplayer game.If you're looking for a game that has the most unstable servers known to man, one that breaks after almost every single update, then Apex is for you. If you're looking for broken match making and a ranked system that rewards time investment more than skill, then Apex is for you. If you're looking for low effort, lackluster, and over expensive micro-transactions that are designed to prey on every intricate psychological vulnerability observed in human beings, then Apex is for you.However, if you're looking for a competent battle-royale game to play with your friends and sink tons of hours in, then look no further than Apex Legends. Just be warned, you can and  will  be permanently banned from accessing the game if you so much as have a disagreement with another player in the game, destroying months of in-game time and money invested, with no hope or chanicee of appeal whatsoever and regardless of past behavior or context of said disagreement.

text: You Deserve better - walk away!   Find something better! The game ran it is course, a waste of time at this point.  There are too many issues - they keep stacking up. it is  not about fixing things - but adding stuff to add stuff without any meaning. It is a husk, a machine without a heart, or a factory that produces good-looking items for people who don't need them or are already dead. Most aspects of the game gradually degrade from the point of recovery - nobody gives a damn if players have fun. To be put on a leash and grind is your fate - only to get a meaningless rank that will reset so that a circle of suffering can continue. Tread in the waters of cheaters, submerge under waves of stacking bugs, hope that your server keeps breathing while you scramble with a rag-tag team, and hope they won't use a mic or don't know how to type. It is a battle royale game, indeed, where players of any rank or skill get to play together while climbing to get a badge of honor where preds, masters, diamonds, plats, golds, silvers, bronze, and even rookies can face each other at any point or league - the game holds to the notion and spirit of battle royale.[i] Rookies flee while you can - the sea is vast, and this fish is already stale, and no amount of seasoning can make it any more edible. The fish is on life support, but that rotten smell gives it away. [/i]  It was a great game, a solid one - time to put it to rest!

text: "Apex Legends: From Noob to Noober"Rating: ★★★☆☆As a relatively fresh-faced Apex Legends enthusiast with a mere 300 hours of gameplay, I've reached that point where I'm no longer considered a "noob." text: A Thrilling Battle royale Experienicee with Some CaveatsRating: 8/10Apex Legends is a game that offers a thrilling and engaging experienicee, especially when you're playing with friends. The gameplay is fun and intense, but it can be frustrating when paired with random teammates who lack tactical awareness. Here's a breakdown of my thoughts:Gameplay Experienicee (8/10): Apex Legends shines when you're grouped with friends or players who understand the game's tactical nature. Playing with random teammates can lead to frustrating losses, and I wish the matchmaking would separate groups from solo players. Despite this, the game is undeniably fun and addictive.Graphics and Visuals (9/10): Running on an RTX 3070 Laptop edition, the game's visuals are stunning. The colors and effects create an immersive experienicee, even if they aren't always the most tactical.Character Selection (9/10): The character selection is diverse and well-balaniceed. I enjoy playing as Ballistic and Ash, but every Legend feels unique and viable. The balanicee is there, even if the learning curve might be steep for some.Community and Multiplayer Interaction (7/10): Like many multiplayer games, the community can be a mixed bag. Communication and cooperation enhanicee the experienicee, but toxicity can be an issue.Performanicee and Optimization (8/10): The game runs smoothly, with occasional server lag. The optimization is generally good, making for a seamless experienicee.Content and Updates (9/10): Regular updates, new characters, and map changes keep the game fresh and exciting. The developers are committed to providing new content, and it shows.Monetization and In-Game Purchases (6/10): While not pay-to-win, EA's monetization model can feel heavy-handed. Expect to spend a significan not amount if you're after heirlooms and premium skins.Overall Satisfaction (8/10): Apex Legends is a fantastic game that offers hours of enjoyment, especially if you have friends to team up with. The frustrations with random teammates and monetization hold it back from perfection, but it is  still a must-play for Battle royale enthusiasts.

text: All the "One Piece" Lore you need to know before you start wasting your time with this atrocity of a game:Much of what we know about the history of the "One Piece" world begins with the so-called Void Century, which began around the year 600. Throughout it, a civilization known today only as the "Great Kingdom" dominated the planet, but by it is end, major power shifts had occurred. According to the scholarly Professor Clover, the Great Kingdom was destroyed in a war with the 20 Kingdoms, which is now known as the World Government, leaving only the mysterious Poneglyphs behind. With the destruction of the Great Kingdom, several kings joined forces to censor the history of the Void Century, outlawing any research on the Poneglyphs.Around two centuries before Monkey D. Luffy's adventures on the Grand Line, the World Government allied with Fish-Man Island following centuries of hate and secured a peace agreement by outlawing slavery. Then, about 30 years prior to the start of the series, Gol D. Roger discovered he had a fatal disease and entered the Grand Line for his final journey. At the same time, Nico Olvia embarked on a Poneglyph expedition, leaving Ohara and her daughter Robin behind. Approximately five years later, the oracle Shyarly's father left her in the care of 15-year-old Arlong, and she predicted the Great Pirate Era as well as the coming of a Mermaid Priniceess.What followed was a three-day battle between the Rogers and Whitebeards, with Oden joining the Rogers. When a man refused to labor in the weapons factory, his execution led to an attack on the Flower Capital by Oden's samurai, and Oden dreamed of opening Wano Country's borders. Later, the Rogers arrived at Laugh Tale, having conquered the entire Grand Line. Gol D. Roger was thus made the Pirate King, and he sent his crew off to enjoy peaceful lives. The pirate world was shaken to the core when the Pirate King, Gol D. Roger, was senteniceed to death by the Marines. Before meeting his end, Roger gave a speech that teased the location of his fabled treasure, the One Piece. This is how the Great Era of Pirates began. Nico Olvia, meanwhile, was freed by the Marines and returned to Ohara and her daughter Robin. Robin was the only survivor when the government assassins later burned Ohara to the ground, so she went on the run.20 years prior to the start of the series, the folk hero Oden tried to open up Wano Country, but was executed. His wife Toki used magic to send a crew 20 years into the future. At the same time, Boa Haniceock and her sisters were enslaved by a Celestial Dragon. A few years later, Queen Otohime began a petition among the merfolk to coexist peacefully with the World Government.Around a decade and a half before "One Piece" kicked off, Fisher Tiger (after several years of enslavement by the Celestial Dragons) struck out for Fish-Man Island. 12 months later, he scaled the Red Line and started a rebellion, aiming to free all the slaves. They reclaimed their slavery marks as marks of the sun, forming the Sun Pirates. A few years later, Shachi and Penguin's parents died in a tsunami, leaving them to be raised in a life of crime by Shachi's aunt and unicele. text: Apex has sadly fallen from it is former glory. Season 0 and 1 were the absolute peak months of this game, primarily because the ORIGINAL (not current) devs that created the game from the OG titanfall team was still working on the game at the time, before they moved on to Gravity Well Studios, another new studio created by a large chunk of the titanfall 1 and 2 devs. Whatever dev team is working on this game now is so disconnected with it is community that both casual and dedicated hardcore players alike get screwed over every single season.Audio bugs? Been a problem sinicee season 0 and you still have abilities, movement, and in some rare occasions vertical audio being absolutely silent. Sometimes even doors opening/closing/breaking makes no sounds. Don't worry, it is  been 'fixed' many times according to Respawn.Anti-cheat? Pointless. Cheaters are in every game and especially competitive ones. Apex is no different with closet cheaters still running rampant. Sometimes it is difficult to tell because of how powerful controller are, but any veteran player can tell a cheater from a controller player at a glanicee.Those $150 heirloom weapons that used to be unique to each character? How about another recolor for the same price and different extra items in it? Sure it usually means either a rework or added animations on an heirloom but why not just have it be an unlockable for previous owners? Easy answer. MONEY.A new event with skins? How about 90% total being recolors for either the same or a more expensive price. Discounted crafting so you can get the shiny heirloom recolors or mythic armor skins? Nah, give us your wallet.A gun releasing in such a powerful state that respawn makes zero changes to it outside of throwing it into the replicator when it is clearly a care package weapon? (Nemesis)Ranked rewards? Instead of giving more rewards how about TAKING away rewards? Dive trails are no longer permanent and ranked trails are basically gone. Why? Because fake predators who don't actually belong in that rank keep their trails on in unranked lobbies thinking they are the best of the best, get griefed and dropped on by half the lobby and complain to the devs about toxicity. it is  not hard to just togood gamele your own dive trail off if you don't want that to happen. Oh but heres ranked frames that no one is going to pay attention to anyways to replace it and those dive trails are now $150 each, locked behind prestige skins.Ranked progression? Always too easy or too grindy and no in-between. You are never really playing with your actual shown rank now because the game matches you using hidden MMR anyways, which also determines how much LP you gain versus killing another player. You can be fighting another diamond of the same rank but get close to no points because their hidden MMR is lower than yours. Respawn also hid showing teammates and opposing players ranked because "it was a bug and not intended to show other players ranks". Yeah no, they just don't want to keep exposing their garbage ranked system that has been bad sinicee it is implementation. Respawn keeps trying to solve the 'ratting' problem but it still ends up being the most effective way of gaining points due to the battle royale nature of the game, and how they designed the LP system in the current season. (season 17)Unranked lobbies are not casual friendly and due to how little rewards ranked has, it pushes all the highly skilled players into unranked, wiping lobbies without any contest whatsoever. This makes the average new player frustrated or immediately quitting altogether. The matchmaking system is also heavily punishing, as one or two good games will easily throw you against players with thousands of hours on the game.Maps? Lets have way too many with only two maps that have gotten major reworks over the entire games lifespan. (Kings Canyon and World's Edge) Olympus only had minor reworks while Storm Point and Broken Moon never had significan not changes to make the maps better. Don't like a map in rotation? Too bad, you have to wait until it switches.Remember arenas? Instead of refining the game mode to make it better, respawn removed it entirely in favor for their other modes thrown into their 'mixtape' playlist, a set shuffling playlist. Want to play only a certain mode? Have to wait until it goes on rotation.Overall gun balanicee? Basically the same guns being used sinicee launiceh with hop ups or gun power being shuffled around from being in care package or not. Kraber is the only gun that has never left the care package sinicee launiceh.Legend balanicee? All over the place. Major reworks on legends either make a character more usable or still bad. (looking at you mirage) Some characters receive only minor buffs or nerfs.Aim assist on controllers? Easily the strongest out of any other fps that has it. The controller aims for you. Mouse and keyboard has superior movement but can not stop you from getting headshot beamed by low TTK guns. Due to the high aim assist, many guns that require a slight degree of muscle memory for shooting turn into hitscan machines with a controller.Pro scene? it is  an absolute mess and good luck trying to get anywhere when a large chunk of tournaments are just easily manipulated kill tournaments or ones that aren't recognized by AlGS. (meaning even if you win said tournaments it does not inicerease your odds at getting invited to ALGS) Respawn added private lobbies way too late and due to that it culled interest for other orgs hosting tournaments.Weapon masteries? What if holding a gun and not using it gives MORE xp than actively doing damage or getting knocks/assists/kills with it? That is the current weapon mastery system in a nutshell.You would think that after so long the game would be in a better state but no, it is quite the opposite. The only real reason why apex has the numbers it does is because there isn't anything that can compete with it 'yet' out of all the BRs that have any kind of esports scene. Respawn has also shown that they only care about the money the game makes and nothing else. Apex is a game that only dedicated players can get any remote enjoyment out of. New players or non dedicated players are better off looking elsewhere for time invested versus reward.As someone who has over 3k hours (steam and origin combined) and played in scrims many times before, Apex is just not a healthy battle royale to invest time into.

text: Apex Legends is a battle royale game that has shown a fair amount of growth sinicee it is release in 2019. Most battle royale genre games have fallen in popularity over the last couple of years but Apex Legends still holds a strong fan base. This game builds off the Titanfall universe for characters and story but combines it to a battle royale game with abilities for each character you play. When starting off, you get a fair amount of characters but it will take a long time before you earn all of them for free. Sinicee this game is published through EA, it is  not surprising to see micro transactions being littered across the game with apex packs, collection events and unlocking characters and weapon skins. Sinicee the game is free to play, this does not bother me as much because it is  completely optional and you can earn characters and apex packs for free, just not as many. It is likely that if you invest time in the game, you will feel like investing money too.The gameplay for Apex Legends is pretty decent. Considering that this game is live service, there are constant balanicee changes and updates with new characters and weapons to shake the meta up each season. Most characters have their strengths and weaknesses which is up to the player to decide which legend suit is them best. Here are my personal thoughts on what Apex is good at and bad at.Pros:- Has constant updates and changes to make the game more interesting- Has a long term grind to work on with unlocking characters and levelling up to the max (level 500)- Has fun moments with friends and is more fun when you take the game less seriously- LTMs during different times of the year are very fun to play & usually have good iniceentive to earn rewards from playing them (with character/weapon skins, trackers or badges)Cons:- Matchmaking (in terms of waiting time) is quite long sometimes & can be frustrating- Matchmaking (in terms of who you match against in matches) has been changed a ton which is making it seem like you are punished for playing well in public matches because you will immediately be matched with iniceredibly sweaty players next game- Ranked has seen a lot of changes to balanicee how LP is earned (a point system based on match performanicee) and this has seen huge issues in the past with players simply camping in zone to earn LP up to master/predator ranks- There is no playlist activity for solo vs solo players, you are stuck with duo or trio due to the fact that Apex Legends is a teamworking-based battle royale. It would be nice to see a solo activity for players.Overall, Apex Legends is a pretty well thought out game with plenty of content to play for no cost. There are times where I have personally felt burnt out from playing due to the nature of the game being a battle royale. it is  definitely worth a try if you are interested in this genre, but I wouldn't try it if you aren't into battle royale games.

Logood gameing into the game, it'll automatically send you to a server with the lowest packet loss, regardless of how far away that server is from you. Freaking Among Us has better servers than Apex currently does.The audio in this game is also horrendous. Each legend has their own unique footstep sounds, making it easier to figure out who is approaching you, which would be extremely handy if the audio in this game would work. 98% of the time you will die to someone walking up behind you with footsteps quieter than a mouse.But the ultimate reason I can't recommend this game to anyone right now is because of how badly Respawn and EA have butchered their rank system. Before, rank went off of what they called "Kill Points", which eniceouraged players to go out into gun fights and take risks to climb through the ranks. Now, in Season 17 they've changed it to what they like to call "Ladder Points", so that you gain more points the higher you place each match. It is a Battle royale after all, being the last team alive out of 60 teams is pretty important, and while I would agree with that statement, it has unfortunately broken ranked, allowing players to camp, sit in corners and avoid all gun fights until the last possible zone. I'm not joking when I say that you can come second place, not do a single bit of damage and get more LP than someone who demolished the whole lobby. Because of this, the average rank you can be this season is the highest rank in the game (Masters), which is insane in a game whose average player base's actual skill level is mid ranks.Overall, Apex Legends is a great game, and I still play it, but sinicee season 13 I can not bring myself to grind like I did because of all the on going issues. Respawn is just another money hungry company who pumps out "events" (just new skins for you to buy, no actual new content) with horrible servers. But hey, if they ever fix the above 3 issues, I'll change it from a no to yes.

Oil and line the base of two 18cm sandwich tins. Sieve the flour, cocoa powder and bicarbonate of soda into a bowl. Add the caster sugar and mix well.STEP 2Make a well in the centre and add the golden syrup, egood games, sunflower oil and milk. Beat well with an electric whisk until smooth.STEP 3Pour the mixture into the two tins and bake for 25-30 mins until risen and firm to the touch. Remove from oven, leave to cool for 10 mins before turning out onto a cooling rack.STEP 4To make the icing, beat the unsalted butter in a bowl until soft. Gradually sieve and beat in the icing sugar and cocoa powder, then add enough of the milk to make the icing fluffy and spreadable.STEP 5Sandwich the two cakes together with the butter icing and cover the sides and the top of the cake with more icing.

text: Coobad game, great mechanics. Love the team aspect of this game. A few problems that need to be address is the fact that rank is somehow easier to play than the regular game mode. It is filled with stacked teams that play this game like they are playing hardcore airsoft. Another issue is the ranking system itself, you can be in a silver and gold lobby in ranked and somehow still run into a full stack of masters and diamond players. This game is not for casual gaming anymore. Back in season 8-9 is where it really just became a place for competitiveness and toxicity. One last thing, I mentioned the team aspect of the team. If you get a good crew together than you can usually shoot for top five; however, a lot of people like to abandon the matches early onicee they get downed, or they won't work with you and you end up 1v3 or 2v3. So while I love this game and I love the entire storyline that's come from it (Devs please stop focusing on revanant and focus on someone else, please.) text: Du sang, des larmes, les skins flashysVoici: Apex Legends Apex Legends est un Battle royale, genre bien définit depuis plusieurs années et orné de plusieurs titres qui, pour la plupart, sont morts.Un genre qui divise énormément car il pousse, à mon sens, à try hard énormément même en casual ce qui peut dégoûter bon nombre de joueurs.Apex Legends ne fait pas exception, le titre est hyper compétitif même en casual, les maps sont grandes, les persos ont des pouvoirs à connaître, chaque arme ne se joue pas de la même manière et cerise sur le gâteau, il faut une gestion d'inventaire quasiment irréprochable.Ayant commeniceé Apex récemment, j'aimerais donner quelques conseils aux débutants qui, comme moi, ont laniceé le jeu pour la première fois et se sont demandés: "C'est quoi cette merde ?".Premier point: Vos settings.En effet sur Apex comme pour beaucoup de FPS battle royale ou non, il est important d'avoir des settings solides notamment en terme de sens souris et FOV. Le FOV scaling qui est une immense connerie est à retirer d'office. Pareil, essayez de régler au maximum vos paramètres afin d'avoir le plus de FPS possible (je suis sur du 165 stable, le jeu est plutôt bien opti).Les serveurs sont aussi corrects, en tout cas je n'ai jamais eu de soucis qui venaient directement d'eux, c'est plus ma co qui est complètement aux fraises.Second Point: Les mécaniques.Apex est né de Titanfall, connu pour ses mécaniques de mouvement assez poussées, permettant wall running, glissades, saut propulsé... . Même si le wall running n'est pas présent sur Apex, vous pouvez néanmoins grimper sur la majeure partie des surfaces, utilisez donice cette verticalité à votre avantage tout le temps.Pareil, courir avec son arme "rentrée" (par défaut touche 3) vous permettra un petit boost de vitesse non négligeable, alternez aussi course et glissades afin d'être plus dur à toucher et de gagner en vitesse.Vous pouvez pratiquer tout ceci dans le stand de tir, et je recommande vivement d'y passer un moment afin de se familiariser avec le point suivant.Troisième point: Les armes.Vous avez plusieurs familles d'armes: Pistolets, Snipers, Fusils de Scouts, Fusils à Pompes, SMG, LMG et AR.Au sein de ces différentes familles vous avez eniceore des sous catégories en fonicetion des munitions qui seront donice Énergétiques, Lourdes, Légères, Munitions de précision ou de fusil à pompe.Chaque arme a aussi le droit d'avoir des équipements qui vont améliorer la maniabilité de cette dernière, il faut donice garder l’œil ouvert afin de repérer les équipements voulus.Le stand de tir est l'endroit idéal afin de se familiariser avec ces armes et de ne pas être complètement perdu en combat, qui sera d'ailleurs le point suivant.Quatrième point: Se taper (la Bagarre).En effet, Apex reste avant tout un FPS, si vous voulez passer 25h à pister des talibans en plein Afghanistan sans rien faire d'autre, il faudra plus se tourner vers du Arma.Apex offre des mécaniques de tir plutôt plaisantes et sans trop de prise de tête, la plupart des armes fonicetionnent en "mag dump", en gros tu vises le mec sans relâcher le clic et t'espères l'OS. Et je ne vous conseille que vivement de vous taper le plus possible au début du jeu, chaque map possède des zones "chaudes", là où une très grosse partie des joueurs vont drop afin de se bagarrer plutôt que de faire une rando.Ces zones chaudes sont à repérer (en gros là où tout le monde va au début) et il faut y drop.Y aller c'est: S'entraîner à mettre des patates à des gens, s'entraîner à tirer sur des gens, s'entraîner à utiliser le kit (pouvoirs) du perso, s'entraîner à gérer son inventaire le plus vite possible, s’entraîner à repérer ce qui est intéressant ou non et entraîner son awareness et se familiariser avec le son.Les "zones chaudes" sont hyper importantes pour les nouveaux joueurs et même si au début vous allez vous faire farmer, retenez avant tout que c'est un BR. Drop à l'autre bout de la map, full loot 2 armes pour se faire instagib par 2 nolife qui ont full farm les mecs à la hotzone, bah c'est pas non plus ce qu'il y a de plus fun. Vous avez nettement plus de chanicee de battre des gens forts quand ils n'ont pas d'armes que quand ils ont un kraber avec une R99 full violet.Cinquièment point: Le CrossPlatform.Alors, un point un peu étrange car pas tellement un conseil, mais il fait l'objet d'une énorme controverse. Au début je me disais "bordel de merde je me fais exploser par des types qui jouent manette, internet disait donice vrai". En fait non.Après avoir testé l'aim assist manette, je suis bien content d'être clavier souris. L'aim assist n'est pas si abusé que ce que les gens veulent vous faire croire et au final, pour ma part c'était un cas de nouveauté et de mauvaise compréhension des mécaniques, mais pour beaucoup je pense que c'est en effet un sévère cas de skill issue.D'accord peut-être que vraiment quand le canon d'une CAR ou R99 est enfoniceé dans votre narine gauche, l'aim assist va faire gagner le duel au mec à la manette. Mais à part ça vous pouvez réellement gagner tous les duels que vous prenez, il suffit de bien vous déplacer et de ne pas engager n'importe comment.Enfin bon, la finalité de tout ceci c'est que vous preniez du plaisir à laniceer ce jeu. Pour ma part j'aime beaucoup, il faut garder en tête qu'il reste hyper compétitif, que beaucoup de mecs sont giga forts dessus et iront vous laser avant que vous ne preniez votre seconde inspiration. Pour ceux qui veulent commeniceer maintenant, même si vous êtes habitué aux FPS, n'y allez pas en mode "C'est bon, chui trop fort sur MW 2019, je vais manger les mecs d'Apex" parce que vous allez ragequit en traitant tout le monde de cheater ou de "putain de joueur console de merde".Allez-y avec un mindset nouveau, réapprenez des choses que vous savez déjà, repartez de 0 et amusez vous.Merci à ceux qui ont lu, je vous souhaite plein de top 1.

text: First of all, let's talk about the graphics. Are you kidding me? I've seen better visual content in a PowerPoint presentation from the '90s. Everything is just so...so...average. There's no "wow" factor. No moment where you think, "Oh my God, this is amazing!"Don't even get me started on the characters. They're like a rejected ensemble cast from a Saturday morning cartoon. Who thought it was a good idea to put a robot, a soldier, and some kind of witch doctor all in the same game? There's no cohesiveness, no unified theme. What's the point?And oh, the mechanics. Yawn. it is  like they borrowed every standard feature from every other battle royale game and mashed it all together. There's nothing unique. The ping system? So what? it is  like using emojis to communicate. Revolutionary? I think not.Loot, loot everywhere and not a drop to drink. Yeah, there are weapons, armor, and gear scattered all around, but good luck finding something useful before getting shot in the back by some 12-year-old with an itchy trigood gameer finger. No tutorial, no guidanicee, just thrown into the deep end. And don't even mention the microtransactions. You might as well just toss your wallet into a black hole.The map design? Please. it is  like someone dumped a bucket of Lego pieces onto a board and said, "Yep, that's our battleground." No strategy, no rhyme or reason, just chaos.And the community? Good luck finding a teammate who isn't either screaming profanities or playing background music so loud you can't hear yourself think. Teamwork is as rare as a unicorn in this game.In summary, Apex Legends feels like a hastily-assembled patchwork quilt of other, better games. it is  uninspiring, unoriginal, and most unforgivingly, un-fun. Would not recommend.(Note: This is a deliberately terrible review and not an accurate representation of Apex Legends, which has been well-received by both players and critics for various aspects like it is diverse characters, smooth mechanics, and unique additions to the battle royale genre.)

text: First things first, the character selection screen is like a "wattson" candy store. With legends as colorful as a "mirage" and abilities that "pathfinder" their way to victory, picking the right legend is like choosing the perfect "caustic" coniceoction. Just remember, "revenant" or later, you'll find the one that suit is you.Now, the map is an "octane"-ic thrill ride, with "horizon"-tal vistas that'll take your breath away. But don't get too "crypto"-graphic with your landing spot, or you'll be in for a real "gibraltar" of trouble. Sometimes you'll find yourself "wraith"-ing through enemy fire, and other times you'll be "bloodhound"-ing for loot like a true treasure "pathfinder."The weapons are a "havoc" of fun, from "r-99" to "kraber," there's a "peacekeeper" for every situation. Just remember to "spitfire" responsibly and try not to "wingman" it when you're under fire. And if things get too "devotion"-al, don't be afraid to "volt" out of there.Communication is key, and the ping system is your best "crypto"-graphy tool. You can "ping" enemies, "ping" loot, and even "ping" where you want to "land-ber." it is  like speaking a universal language that even a "revenant" could understand.But sometimes, despite your best efforts, you'll find yourself in a real "caustic" situation, with no "respawn" in sight. Don't "fear" though; just "revenant" and try again! And when you finally get that "victory royale," it'll be like hitting the "lottery" of gaming moments.In summary, Apex Legends is a "crypto"-nomic blast filled with enough pun-tastic action to make even a "pathfinder" smile. it is  a "mirage" of fun, and if you haven't given it a shot, you're "loba"-ing out on a legendary experienicee. So, grab your "wraith"-y sense of humor and dive into the "octane"-ic world of Apex Legends!

The game's weapons feel satisfying to use, and the inicelusion of attachments and mods adds further depth to combat. The addition of a ping system simplifies communication, making it easy to coordinate with teammates even without voice chat.Graphics and Sound (9/10):Visually, "Apex Legends" is a treat. With a commitment to engaging lore, diverse characters, and intense firefights, "Apex Legends" is a game that has set a high standard for the genre. text: Gather round little ones, it is  time for a story.Our tale begins, of course, in the lobby, provided you even managed to connect to the servers and reach it in the first place. Hitting the connect button, you shortly join a game, and start selecting your legend. Perhaps you're picking first, and you go for Pathfinder; he's got fun dialogue lines and you like playing him. Then it is  your first squadmate's turn. You watch with growing disappointment as the bar whittles away until the game autoselects their last played legend. it is  a Wraith. That's fine, you tell yourself, maybe the third person will play another class. Well, little one, prepare for another round of disappointment as the same thing happens again, except this time, lo and behold, it is  an Octane. Wonderful. The screens change, and soon you're all in the dropship. You relinquish Jumpmaster sinicee you don't want that responsibility. Then you remember who your teammates are playing and realise, panicking, what a grave mistake you just made. The Wraith decides to hotdrop solo, and you're left with the Octane. He decides to drop soon after the Wraith and picks another hotdrop. Except rather than just press w and drop fast, he takes his time, maybe make a turn there, ooh how about I change my mind and go that way, no wait a minute I'll turn back the way we were going in the first place. Not to mention that the Wraith who hotdropped solo is long dead and disconnected. Eventually the two of you land, long after everyone else has dropped and started looting. it is  no great surprise that another squad immediately starts shooting at you two, you run and try to find a gun, while the Octane stims and runs around in circles like a headless chicken. Lucky you, you manage to snag yourself a grand haul of a p2020 and a charge rifle. The Octane gets downed and then killed, but somehow you manage to take out the squad and grab his banner. You make your way to the respawn beacon and start the timer. But wait, would you look at that! With about 0.2 seconds to go, the Octane left. You know what, kudos to him for not leaving right after getting knocked. Oh what the hell, you think, deciding to stick around. Well, that won't last long, as you're shortly obliterated and sent back to the lobby. And of course, knowing that the same scenario is going to repeat itself over and over, you hit the connect button onicee again.

text: Generally a good game, the slightly faster gameplay compared to other battle royales makes it more interesting though time to kill is in my opinion a little too high still. The legends are quite balaniceed with all their unique perks but considering you either have to play around 25h to unlock another one if you don't decide to purchase them for real money the game does get a bit stale when it comes to picks as almost everyone uses the ones you start with. As with all battle royales, a fight or game depends on lots of RNG of spawned loot, zone movement and other things outside of your control in situations leading to not so satisfying results in moments though this is generally not a problem. Though this can be made up by the fact that you can respawn teammates with relative ease, so fleeing can be an option.When it comes to skill and learning the curves are quite long and steep at times with all the ways you can play and the very differing weapons. The most relevant skill being a steady aim and tracking ability of course but also movement skills of verying degree like tap-strafing and abusing other stuff related to the source engine. Fights are also heavily dependent of people close to you as 3rd partying is extremely effective in this game with the high movement and special abilities. The only real gripe I have with this game is the UI design which is unnecessarily cluttered in many places leaving you to search for things that should be intuitive to find but isn't with so many sub menus and the design language of the game.Performanicee wise it is  quite good, I'm getting relatively stable 170fps @1440p max settings across all maps with a RTX 3070ti / R7 5800x3D combo. Though fire do give a few frametime hitches when it is  close in your face, something probably related to running on a modified Source™ engine as CS:GO and CS2 both has had these problems as well. With the game being run by EA you need an account on their platform even if nothing else have to launiceh when playing. And again being run by EA means lots of monetization which is very prevalent in the menu with there being shops, lootboxes, battlepasses, characters you have to either grind hundreds of hours for or buy them directly combined with stuff like ads for the battlepass and shop in-game.Overall the gameplay feels good and like a combination of other games with it being a battle royale, hero shooter, fps with fast movement for a battle royale but in the end it comes together quite satisfyingly. Though with there being no solo mode I'd have to recommend only playing with other people as going 1v2 or even 1v3 and then getting backstabbed even if you win the first fight makes the game feel unfair.

text: Having played Apex Legends on Origin (now EA App), I must say it is  a truly impressive Battle royale game. The gameplay is of remarkably high quality, deliveryng an enjoyable and fun experienicee. Although my playtime has been limited, I thoroughly enjoyed what I experieniceed.The core gameplay mechanics are well-polished, providing smooth and engaging combat that stands out in the Battle royale genre. The game's emphasis on team-based play and the inicelusion of unique character abilities, known as Legends, adds a layer of depth and strategy that sets it apart from other titles in the genre.While Apex Legends excels in many aspects, I did find the loot system to be a bit awkward. Although it does not hinder the overall enjoyment of the game, it could benefit from further refinement and improvement. However, this is a minor criticism in comparison to the game's overall strengths.In conicelusion, Apex Legends is undeniably a great addition to the Battle royale genre, boasting high-quality gameplay and innovative mechanics. Despite having played only a limited amount, I found myself thoroughly enjoying the experienicee. With some minor improvements to the loot system, this already fantastic game has the potential to become even more exceptional. I highly recommend giving Apex Legends a try, especially if you're a fan of the Battle royale genre or enjoy team-based gameplay with unique character abilities.
label: TRUE

text: Honest Apex Review Playtime500 Hours PC (MNK)2000 Hours Console (ROLLER) Rating 7/10Positives - Good Movement (Feels just right and rewarding imo for understanding the mechanics) - Pretty Balaniceed (You can get value from anybody really and any pick is fine if you aren't playing in tournaments) - Gives A Fair Amount Of Free Rewards (Compared to other ftp games, Apex honestly gives you a lot of free stuff just for investing your time, you can easily multiple gold skins, and even craft them without spending anything on the game.)- Good Lore (Lore between characters is interesting and gives them depth, however leakers tend to kill out any hype by showing important things off early) - Promotes Diversity (Each legend is diverse in shape, race, gender, sexuality, culture and promotes this healthily) Negatives - Lack Of Game modes (Season 3 was honestly one of the best seasons because of how many game modes it had. Please add more gamemodes, or a gamemode rotation, or improve upon mixtape. - Gets Repetitive (Easy to burnout because content is honestly lack luster)- Overpriced Cosmetics (Every F2P game has this issue, however Respawn and EA tends to release recolours of a skin at max price, even in collection events. Which is stupid) - Legend Tokens are Useless (I have like over a million and there's barely anything to spend them on) - Terrible Matchmaking (Matchmaking is awful for solo queue players, however ranked matchmaking is honestly a lot better and feels less sweaty than pubs even though it is  the "competitive mode". - Constantly removing Movement tech (Probably a hot take but honestly I hate when movement is taken out because it removes a degree of skill expression). - Lack Of Cross-progression/Merging (It may come soon but I've been waiting for so long I honestly just gave up playing the game) - Ranks Feel Like They Don't Matter Anymore (The direction of ranked is honestly good imo, however currently ranks feel like they don't matter at all this season. I feel like a Bronze player is worth the same as Plat now and there's no notable differenicee. Might just be me though) - Why Does Sydney have so many servers? (I haven't been able to play Duos sinicee Season 4 because there is no players) Also Sydney players also have to tend to switch to other servers in higher ranks because the queues are so slow. Why are there 5 Sydney server options when the playerbase is so low? Wouldn't it be better to have less servers so each server is more populated and queue times are faster? Also wouldn't it save money because there is less servers they would have to pay for? I'm no expert or anything but if this is how it works please do it because the queues are so bad here) Awful Audio Playback (it is  been an issue for so long, pretty sure it is  server sided though. I just wish footsteps were just as consistent as Gunshots lol) Final ThoughtsI do enjoy this game I just have no reason to play anymore, I have barely touched the game for a year and every time I play I just don't feel motivated to play. I have no reason to grind on a worse account and there is no real content apart from a buniceh of overpriced recolours thrown in my face.

text: Honestly the game itself is . . . fine. it is  a Battle royale game. It even has some novel characters, story, and team-based play. So why the thumbs down? Simply put, on a technical level I've seldom eniceountered such an ill-fitted mess. Last time was Cyberpunk 2077. Control issues, connection issues, graphic runtime issues, clipping problems, invisible walls, and more are all dreadfully apparent - though the issues appear to apply equally across players, so it does not mess too badly with the skill. If this was a newly released game, I'd give it an easy pass for growing pains. But after years? And with EA behind it? It feels like under a different company this could have dethroned Fortnite. Instead, key parts of gameplay outside of the Battle royale itself are mostly left behind stingy paywalls. After killing their introduction packages for new players, I wouldn't be surprised if this games days were numbered.To round out my criticisms, here's what I think the game does exceptional at. It has a great non-verbal communication system. It has great consistenicey with adding new content. It has some neat experimental character types. it is  just a pity it does not get to shine.TLDR: Do you need to play a Battle royale with friends and don't want Fornite? it is  probably this - But keep your expectations low. Everyone else can find better games to play - though there is no shame in playing this .

text: I am taking a break from the game right now but think it is time to finally leave a review. I will not say anything about the shop though, as it´s just what f2p games are like and naturally isn´t a thing to be desired.You will find a lot of people complaining about the game that´s mostly just them being burned out like me after playing the game for years on end so my own review might a bit biased in that regard.But still, I have to say the game is amazing, otherwise I wouldn´t have played it for almost 2k hours (started on the origin client).The movement isn´t titanfall but if you ever play another fps after this, it will feel slow and janky. It´s just so fluent and well done in Apex Legends without having the massive skill requirement you will find in tf2.The weapons are probably the most balaniceed ones you will ever find in any game and the time to kill is just right. Every weapon is very usable and viable in a fight (except the p2020 maybe). The legends are fun and diverse gameplaywise. Few things broken here and at the end of the day it´s a shooter, which means that your choice of legend will rarely be the only reason you win a fight.If you are interested in ranked, you are in for a ride though. The devs are experimenting a lot and often those games will be a chore with no reward, rather than a fun way to test your skill. The pub lobbies are also very sweaty and have the same leaver problem as any other game.All in all, although there are a lot of shortcomings, Apex Legends is still a very very fun movement shooter, no matter what people say and the content drought is not as bad as those tiktok attention span crybabies sugood gameest. It´s definetly worth checking out, if you are interested. It´s free after all

If you showed me an Apex Legends character, I wouldn't be able to differentiate them from pretty much any modern shooter. text: I hate:The gun models take up almost half the screen and when you reload it is  even more, which you have to do all the time because nerds deemed this a "mechanic". does not matter if it is  a bad mechanic that's not even necessary because it is  a virtual world, any mechanic will do because it is  more than simply holding down the controller button.The guns also sway a lot, it is  very distracting and annoying and unnecessary. it is  as annoying to me as interactive pop-up ads on the web, except even they don't happen as frequently as having to reload in this reloading simulator.Many of the guns are extremely inaccurate if you're not ADS'ing, I don't understand why. I'm guessing it is  to please a certain crowd, but I hate it. Futuristic supersoldiers sliding down slopes and jumping from 100m without taking damage, somehow they can't handle guns and have to come to a near stop to be able to aim them.Battle royale is a failed system. Instead of respawning somewhere, you respawn at the main menu and have to find another game. It also means shallow matches, because it is  just about staying alive until the end. There is no player control over the territory, as the area where you're allowed to be shrinks and the remaining players get pushed closer towards each other. This is because otherwise 1 guy could hide on the other side of the map and the game would last forever because the other remaining player can't find him. Unfortunately, as the territory shrinks further and further, so do the strategic and tactical options and you just end up playing a death match on a larger map with randomized item drop locations. Compared to Planetside (not 2) it is  extremely boring and clearly meant for players with short attention spans.The buildings in this game don't seem to have any funicetion besides cover points. There are no objectives, no benefit is to owning a base or building, and nothing to really indicate what their purpose is from an artistic standpoint. Of course that does not matter when your game tries to push people out of these buildings and item distribution is random, but I think it is  a pretty big waste of opportunity.The items also look like candies with annoying outlines made for easy viewing on television screens while playing from the couch, and the U.I. is the same as every other game trying to appeal to dumb pro-gamers; simple white lines and supposedly quick to understand icons that are too abstract to remember.Compared to Titanfall, the movement is gimped. You can't run on walls, you have no double jump, everything feels slower. Apparently you can pick up a jetpack but if it is  anything like the semi-controlled wingsuit gliding you get when launicehing via a rope then I'd rather play Tribes, it is  vastly superior in terms of control over movement. You can slide down a sloped surface as if frictionless in Apex, except you don't gain any speed and you have to fall down backwards first like a soccer player trying to bait a penalty kick.Stop making these terrible games. Battle royale sucks.

It doesn’t matter if it’s ranked or casual. You can be a Gold and still match up with Bronzers that leads to: Apex was killed when custom squads were able to join solo que lobbies. Yes in every multiplayer game if you play with your friends you are going to do better. When they allowed those players into solo ques lobbies, it destroyed the solo que experienicee so much that you will never match with anyone of similar skill for one and two you will be stomped by these custom teams. In my opinion, custom teams should only que to custom team lobbies. Controller players have asinine levels of aim-assist and you cannot turn off crossplay. Not to mention the game does nothing to address 3rd party devices like Cronus most high ranks are likely to use. This is why sometimes if you watch streamers they will have no recoil whatsoever. When the game decides that, you have had enough losses you will be put into a bot lobby. Bot lobbies feel almost like offline, smoother, no lag. Also in these lobbies, the game will give you an easier aimassist that will constantly throw you off from your normabad game. For example, you get used to the games ‘aimassist’ but in a normal lobby it will be turned off noticeably. I use mouse and keyboard so it was easier for me to notice this.Also not to mention the crazy levels of censorship in Apex. Type anything bad and you will be suspended or banned if repeat offense. text: I have played this game for many years. I have another 1000 hours on origin and 750 hours on steam. The core mechanics, gun play, maps, artwork, new legends, it is  all gotten better over the years. Matchmaking has ruined this game, in every imaginable way. For some reason, I have been classified as some 'good player,' where every single game I go up against pros, predators, 4k/20bombs, masters, and get completely dominated. I've played 500 games this season and not a single one where I felt I was above the average skill level. I'm getting crushed constantly. And usually to add to this, I get paired with someone who is brand new to the game. I can't even imagine how they feel. This game will punish you until you break. It is guaranteed to infuriate you, invalidate progress, and remind you that you are bad. The matchmaking has gotten worse and worse every season, where it is now impossible to have a good time. Even in the casuabad game modes, I get paired against sweaty 3-stacks who play like there is money on the line. I have not had a good time playing this game in a very long time, which makes me tremendously sad. I have loved the TF2 / Apex universe for a long time, and this game has driven me mad to the point where it is  no longer healthy for me to play. I won't even get into the ranked system, because it has been beaten to death this season and I am sure it will get completely reworked (again). Respawn, all I want is to feel rewarded for improving. Not punished. You are driving the core, dedicated base away by crushing them under the weight of pro players in ranked and unranked alike.
label: FALSE

text: I highly recommend this game, even if you don't have friends with practice you will be able to play the game solo, and if you do have fiends then congrats you can play the game as intended, if you chose to pick up apex here's some tips I learned1. by strafing, whether left to right or just in one continuous direction, you can near completely eliminate vertical recoil2.cover is your friend, if you don't have cover you lose the fight, and if you can get your enemy out of cover then you win the fight3. avoid weapons with slow fire rates, such as any of the snipers, shotguns, and LMG's, these weapons aren't bad they are just not beginner friendly 4. if an enemy is close enough don't bother ADS-ing the accuracy of the weapon is the same ADS-ed or not, just keep the enemy in the center of your screen and you'll hit your shots5. don't worry about which legend is "best" as in fight's gun skill is more important, if you go to the firing range you can try out every legend, it is best to main at least 3 legends, all of the starter legends are decent choicesand with that I hope I have helped you in your endeavors, and I hope to see you in the ring, but you won't be seeing me. text: I may have a slight bias towards apex however i will try to be as neutral as possible during this. Apex is a fun game if you are looking for a more hardcore strategy driven battle royale experienicee, however with that comes a steep learning curve. This can be a potential point of issue especially for new players who sometimes don't feel like there's any avenue for them to "get better" for lack of a better term. this game will test your mental stability through every corner.Compared to the time that i've spent playing both fortnite and warzone this game makes me angry more than both of the others combined. Yet i can not seem to stop playing it. The games skill ceiling is extremely high compared to other BR's. Thanks to the games over the top movement mechanics and enticing gun play you find yourself wanting to get better and learn all the things. however they really need to find better solutions for new players.They updated the firing range in season 17. this brought a whole host of ways to practice some of the crazy movement mechanics that are availible. however they do not have the same type of thing for gunfight practice. you can edit the way the bots behaive and their accuracy i believe. however compared to real players in apex, the bots are nothing. This is especially prevalent to new players who feel like they can not learn being thrown into lobbies that have players with 1000+ kills on a single legend. these players probably having well over that amount in lifetime kills and hundreds of more hours sunk into the game than them. it is  very discouraging, it is  why i see most new players leaving (that and the blatant toxicity towards newer players from "OG's").As a player with over 1000 hours in this game and 1300 on console before switching, i try my best to help new players along when i come across them. BECAUSE THERE IS NO GODDAMN REASON A LEVEL 25 PLAYER SHOULD BE IN LOBBIES WITH SOMEONE WHO HAS OVER 2000 HOURS IN THIS GAME. It simply doesnt make any sense, and i fully understand if thats the reason you quit. And as for us "OG's", we feel like were getting screwed by EA and respawn. They don't listen to the people that have played their hearts out in this game. those of us who have spent ungodly amounts of money on this game. they do not listen to a lot of community feedback, unless it is staring them right in the face and are getting bad press for it of course. in conicelusion, this is a good game at it is core. for new players it can be especially frustrating and time consuming to get decent at this game, but do not tlet that discourage you. do not SPEND ANY MONEY ON THE GAME. I recently stopped and it feels a lot better not spending a lot. but i do eniceourage giving a shot at least. it is  free, so can not hurt.
label: TRUE

text: I recently dove headfirst into the exhilarating world of Apex Legends, and boy, oh boy, did I embark on a rollercoaster of emotions (and empty wallets)! This game's got it all - fast-paced action, thrilling battles, and microtransactions that make my bank account scream louder than a Wraith portal.Let's start with the gameplay. Apex Legends is like a high-stakes game of "Who Can Jump the Farthest Off a Cliff?" Except, instead of cliffs, you're jumping out of a dropship with your squad, armed to the teeth, ready to fight for fame, glory, and bragood gameing rights. Nothing like starting your day with a casual 5 a.m. drop into a hot zone and realizing you've forgotten how to aim. Oops!The characters in this game are as unique as they come. Each legend brings something different to the table, from tracking abilities to creating portals that even a confused cat would have trouble resisting. And of course, there's always that one squad mate who insists on playing Caustic and filling the entire battlefield with toxic gas, turning the game into a weirdly agood gameressive air freshener commercial.But here's where things get interesting - microtransactions. Apex Legends is like the universe's way of testing your willpower. You start out strong, vowing never to spend a dime. But then you see that sexy skin for your favorite legend, and suddenly you're shelling out your luniceh money for virtual cosmetics that make you look cooler than a snowman in sunglasses. And if you think you're going to resist the temptation of those shiny loot boxes, think again! They've got you covered with the surprise mechanics, and before you know it, your bank account is gasping for air like it is  drowning in a Gibraltar bubble shield.But wait, there's more! The battle pass is like the carrot on a stick, dangling just out of reach. It promises exclusive rewards, but you'll have to grind like a caffeinated hamster on a wheel to unlock them all. And when you finally get that legendary skin you've been dreaming of, you'll be so exhausted that you'll forget how to aim again. it is  a vicious cycle.In conicelusion, playing Apex Legends is like being on a wild, expensive rollercoaster ride through a virtual warzone. it is  a blast, but your bank account might need some counseling afterward. So, strap in, squad up, and get ready to loot, shoot, and wonder why you just spent your rent money on a digital heirloom. Apex Legends, you beautiful, microtransaction-riddled monster - I can't quit you!

text: I used to love Apex Legends, but after playing it for a few years, I feel like it is  losing it is charm. It seems like the developers are more interested in making money from cosmetics than creating new and exciting content for the game. Let me explain why.First of all, the events are getting boring and repetitive. They are mostly just recolors of existing skins, or skins that look very similar to each other. The collection events are especially disappointing, because they are supposed to be the most prestigious and rare items in the game. But instead of giving us something unique and cool, they just give us a recolored heirloom, which is basically a slap in the face to the players who spent hundreds of dollars to get the original one.Secondly, the seasons are not as hype as they used to be. The new legends are not very balaniceed or fun to play, and the new maps are not very memorable or diverse. The season updates are also very bugood gamey and glitchy, and sometimes they break the game more than they fix it. The only good thing they added recently was the mixtape mode, which has some fun and fast-paced modes like team deathmatch, gun game and control. But even that gets old after a while, because there is not much variety or progression in it.Thirdly, and most importantly, they really need to add cross-progression to the game. This is a feature that allows you to play on any platform with the same account and keep all your progress and items. This is a feature that every other major free-to-play game has, like Fortnite, Warzone, Genshin Impact, etc. This is a feature that should be the norm for online games in 2023. And yet, Apex Legends is still behind on this.I have been playing Apex Legends on console sinicee it launicehed, and I have invested a lot of time and money into it. I have unlocked all the legends, many skins, badges, charms, battlepasses, and heirloom in the game. I have made a lot of friends and memories in this game. But I also want to play it on PC, because I have a good gameing PC that can run it better than my console. I want to enjoy the higher graphics, framerate and performanicee that PC offers. I want to try out different settings and keybinds that suit my playstyle better. I want to join the larger and more competitive PC community.But I can't do any of that, because there is no cross-progression in Apex Legends. If I switch to PC, I will lose everything I have earned on console. I will have to start from scratch, with many of the legends locked, no skins, no badges, no charms, no heirlooms and not have any of the battlepasses I worked to finished. I will have to grind all over again, with no rewards or iniceentives. I will have to pay again for everything I already paid for on console. text: I will be as honest as I can here. This game is overall very fun an satisfying to play. I have had some great times on this game but i have also gotten unbelievably mad at this game as well. This game has not been handled well by the dev team but I see some great potential if a few issues were fixed. Things I like: This game is semi easy to get into, it has a relatively low skill floor but the skill ceiling is crazy high. All the guns other than the snipers feel great to fire. Fights can be very satisfying at times, even when I lose I can still think to myself "Even though I lost, that was still very fun."Things I dislike: The SSBM (skill based match making) is very agood gameressive, I have played well for one game and I get stomped the next 15 games. The loot pool is very bad imo, I have seen ammo spawn next to more ammo with no weapons in sight. I have opened a loot crate and gotten nothing useful at all. The loot pool is my bigood gameest issue with this game by far. The community kinda sucks. I have been called more names in this game than COD. You will get crap talked by randoms that ran off in a random direction and died halfway across the map. The servers suck overall, I have Ethernet and 1 gig internet speed but I will still sometimes get over 100 ping. If you don't live in the US the servers are hit or miss (or at least what I have heard from others). Lastly, there are some huge content droughts that happen at random. There was a stretch of time where the only new content released was just new skins and cosmetics (only stuff that you had to pay for that didn't change the game-play at all). Now this is more about the community than the game, but If you get into a fight you will probably be third partied by another squad, if not multiple enemy squads at the same time.In the end it is an okay game that could be so much better if the devs put more love and care into the game-play and balanicee instead of skins and cosmetics.

text: I will break down the different aspects of the game in sections: Introduction and early experienicee:As soon as you log in for the first time, you have a rudimentary run-through of the basics of game mechanics. The firing range is comprehensive enough that you can adjust parameters to meet certain goals. After the training intro, though, you are given two introductory matches filled with bots and other new players where you can put the things you were taught into practice with the real-game situations. I feel this is lacking, at least in the practical application aspect. Unfortunately, learning the maps is strictly " trial-by-fire." There is no way to run through the maps (without the use of outside applications) and see what is what. It can be an especially difficult barrier to entry for new players that don't have a friend to help guide them through the early game. Otherwise, it ends up with frustration on how to drop, where the hot drops are, and how to loot effectively. Mid Game/late game:Most Battle royales are similar in many coniceepts, so onicee those are learned, the game becomes much more enjoyable. Season depending, there are issues with matchmaking in ranked and in public matches where you are unfairly paired against players of a much higher skill level. I am not a high-tier player and therefore don't have a ton of experienicee in what the higher lobbies are like, I have come across cheaters even in my games. The most frequent abuse I see is "smurfing" where a higher skilled player spoofs the game into thinking they are a lower level player than they are and they easily can defeat the players that are "supposed" to be there. That occurs (as of season 18) about 3-4/10 matches I play in PUBS. Overall, the game is enjoyable for me and allows me to think on my feet and de-stress from work. I would recommend this game for those that enjoy the genre of game and are looking for something a little more engaging than Fortnite. The movement aspect of the game has a high skill ceiling which satisfies the replayable aspect that I look for in my games.

text: I would just chalk this up to Battle royale not really being my thing, but there's other main things that I believe work against the game.Gunplay is a mixed bag. I like the variety of weapons, and as someone who came from titanfall it was nice to see familiar ones. However, the change from mostly hitscan in those games to mostly projectile here is nothing short of a poor choice. Maybe it helps prevent aimbots from auto locking? That does not sound like the best fix by punishing everyone else. Either way, this leads to the "A-D Wigood gamelers" to roam free, tapping away like they're playing a PC port of Danicee Danicee Revolution.The game is heavily RNG based. This seems to be a staple for most Battle royales. But now that other ones like COD's version have introduced the coniceept of loadouts, I really feel this game would benefit from that. Running around with no gun because the game gave you attachments and optics isn't fun, it is  annoying. just something simple like selecting two guns, maybe a magazine or two for each, that's all that's needed. The main selling point is the legends. They're perfectly fine. Some of the personalities are definitely better than others. However a lot of them get ruined (Lifeline, Wraith, Horizon) because of the super toxic crowd that use them sinicee they are more "meta" or "easier to play".There is a very stagnant progression and drip feed of content. Almost too little. And yes there's real money that can fix that. That does not change what is available as a free-to-play person. I should feel like I can buy extra things, not that I HAVE to spend money to enjoy cosmetics. And to top it off, there are non-battle royale modes. However, the game feels like it actively punishes you for playing. Most daily challenges seem to require battle royale to do. Weeklies take longer to do which makes sense, as there's a quicker time to kill, but it feels like a slog. Overall, some great ideas, but bad execution, badder gunplay, and some of the baddest progression systems leads to this being a 9-5 job where you are stuck on the bottom of the ladder for eternity.

text: if you came to my post looking for a good honest review your in the wrong spot but i will show yall how to make banana bread instead.No need for a mixer for this recipe! Clean-up is easy, too. If you want, you can mix everything in one bowl.This recipe calls for an 8- x 4-iniceh loso muchpan. If you are baking in an 8 1/2- x 4 1/2-iniceh pan, bake for 47 to 57 minutes. If baking in a 9- x 5-iniceh pan, bake for 45 to 55 minutes. Note that the larger pan size will produce a flatter loaf.The best bananas to use for banana bread are those that are over-ripe. The yellow peels should be at least half browned, and the bananas inside squishy and browning.Ingredients2 to 3 medium (7" to 7-7/8" long) very ripe bananas, peeled (about 1 1/4 to 1 1/2 cups mashed)1/3 cup (76g) butter, unsalted or salted, melted1/2 teaspoon baking soda (not baking powder)1 piniceh salt3/4 cup (150g) sugar (1/2 cup if you would like it less sweet, 1 cup if more sweet)1 large egood game, beaten1 teaspoon vanilla extract1 1/2 cups (205g) all-purpose flourPreheat the oven and prepare the pan:Preheat the oven to 350°F (175°C), and butter an 8 x 4-iniceh loso muchpan.Mash the bananas and add the butter:In a mixing bowl, mash the ripe bananas with a fork until completely smooth. Stir the melted butter into the mashed bananas.Smashing bananas to make banana bread.Melted butter added to a bowl of mashed bananas.Mix in the remaining ingredients:Mix in the baking soda and salt. Stir in the sugar, beaten egood game, and vanilla extract. Mix in the flour.Sugar mixed with butter and bananas to make banana bread.Flour added to a bowl of wet ingredients to make banana bread.Bake the bread:Pour the batter into your prepared loso muchpan.Banana bread batter poured into a loso muchpan.Kelly HamiltonBake for 55 to 65 minutes at 350°F (175°C), or until a toothpick or wooden skewer inserted into the center comes out clean. A few dry crumbs are okay; streaks of wet batter are not. If the outside of the loso muchis browned but the center is still wet, loosely tent the loso muchwith foil and continue baking until the loso muchis fully baked.Cool and serve:Remove from oven and let cool in the pan for a few minutes. Then remove the banana bread from the pan and let cool completely before serving. Slice and serve. (A bread knife helps to make slices that aren't crumbly.)Wrapped well, the banana bread will keep at room temperature for 4 days. For longer storage, refrigerate the loso muchup to 5 days, or freeze it.Baked banana bread in a loso muchpan.

text: it is  absolutely impossible to enjoy Apex Legends![h2]The Good[/h2]Honestly, the core gameplay of Apex Legends has to be one of the best games out right now. The weapons, the characters, the movement mechanics, everything is deep and enjoyable. If it is  all you look at, and when they truly feel balaniceed, it really is a great game at it is  core, but it is  potential is just ruined by terrible choices of the dev team.[h2]The Bad[/h2]Horrific match making, there is no such thing as casual play. I can't understand it, what am I even playing? I'm a dad, I play a couple hours a week of video games, I just want to hop on with some buddies and get some kills and have a little fun, NOT ALLOWED! You will play against the sweatiest try-hards imaginable EVERY GAME, no breaks.it is  brutal, but not in the fun grinding haha brutal kind of way, more in the draining you of life almost immediately kind of way. If you play regular lobbies, you play against the top 1% multi-thousand kills players, you play ranked, you still play against those same guys! What's the point of either one, I just want to play against players around my skill level, I don't want to just get headshoted back to back by a triple-take from miles away.I don't want to spend 10 minutes looting and looting with no-mic randoms just to be rolled and not even fire my weapon by a full masters squad in pubs. I don't want to get to end game with 10 teams alive and half of them are just rats in the most disgusting rat spots and I just have to wait til final circle and throw a 50/50 shot at who gets a down first. The game has deteriorated to such a point that you have to do the most boring stuff to win, or you die immediately from a third party every gun fight. [h2]The Ugly[/h2]There's so much ugly about this game, the toxic player base, the hacking, the over-powered legends and weapons on release to sell skins, the overpriced store items, the audio, the list is endless. [h2]Conicelusion: STAY AWAY![/h2]Some of my favorite streamers, and yours', play this game and even for them it is  a brutal grind but sometimes it is  impressive what they can achieve and it makes it look like fun. The reality is very far from that, those guys play 8-10 hours every day for years to get that good. Playing this game for the first time, or even casually is absolutely impossible.
