text: It is quite difficult to describe shooting in words, but what can be said for sure is that in every shooter on the market it has it is own and does not repeat other games, except maybe CS:GO and Valorant.Among the features of weapons, it is worth noting the absenicee of normal bolt rifles, do you want to kill in the head with 1 shot as in a conditional Pubg? You are not here. The thing is that the game has a high TTK, and if they killed right away it would introduce a hard imbalanicee.It just so happened that the game was made on Source, and it is physics, like in the Quake engine, has a buniceh of features that allow you to discover amazing things. Although someone is probably not happy with the choice of the game engine, in fact we have the most optimized battle royale on the market, but most importantly, the players have found a dozen features not planned by the developers, which unfortunately are gradually being cut out.At the moment, the Apex has the following movement mechanics:BunnyhopSlideWall jumpTapstrafeSuperglideZipline Super JumpI probably missed something else, but these are the main mechanics, more about each one below.Bunnyhop — The same mechanics straight from speedrunning games, makes it possible to jump like a hare. At the moment, it is very much frayed, but still quite useful, because at high speed it allows you to jump into cover while winding the jar, which is much faster than walking.Slide — The game has the ability to slide, but because of the physics of the engine, it can be done not only forward, but also sideways / backward, and practically from the spot, which allows you to sharply slide sideways even on a flat surface, rather than outplay the opponent.Walljump — Allows you to jump away from the walls. The game has the mechanics of crawling on walls, but if you press jump in the air when touching a wall, the character will jump away from it, which looks quite impressive and often effective, it is  even better than running on walls from Titanfall, because it requires skill and feels much better.Tapstrafe — Makes it possible to dramatically change the direction in the air.Superglide — Instant acceleration after climbing.Zipline Super Jump — A high jump when using a rope.Unfortunately, developers periodically remove or cut mechanics from the game, such as they cut out Punicehboost — the ability to accelerate when sliding by hitting a place under their ass, like how children push off with their hands on a slide to start rolling faster, or penguins, if you will.One of the things that repels many players, because weapons must kill quickly, otherwise it is  not realistic!Is the TTK in Apex the highest compared to other battle royales? Yes, but not as much as many people think.The number of HP in popular grand pianos:Apex Legends — 225 (100 hp + 125 armor)Warzone 2 — 250 (100 hp + 150 armor)Fortnite — 200 (100 hp + 100 armor)In fact, the DPS (Damage per second) of the weapon affects, and yes, in Warzone 2 and Fortnite, it is a little more, but not enough to kill the enemy with a couple of shots, and there and there you will have to land half or even more of the clip. Also, in Apex Legends, your enemies do not always have a red shield with 125 armor, as a rule, it is only in deep leith, which means that most of the time your enemies will have 175-200 hp.Anyone who has not played games with a high TTK — I advise you to try. The higher the TTK, the more personal skill is required to shoot the enemy, especially if we talk about close combat on the PP, in which you need to hit almost every shot, otherwise there simply won't be enough cartridges in the clip.Each character has 3 abilities: passive, active and ultimate. Here you have a robot with a hook-cat, and the opportunity to create your own gas chamber, and a mommy with a black hole.Each character has 3 abilities: passive, active and ultimate. Here you have a robot with a hook-cat, and the opportunity to create your own gas chamber, and a mommy with a black hole.Each match lasts an average of 15-20 minutes, which is much faster than other battle royales, when landing not on the edge of the map, it is likely to keep the pace throughout the game and constantly fight with other players. Not the bigood gameest maps and a lot of mechanics for fast movement contribute to this, although there are times when you can run for 10 minutes and not find anyone, especially in the public.When knocked out, the enemy falls to his knees, but can be covered with a shield that absorbs a lot of damage, which makes it harder to finish him off than in other games.But if you still finished him off, you can instantly take his armor for yourself, while it will be with whole shields, which allows you to change the shield in the heat of battle and win it on this. You can also get shields from corpses to the ground, and if you are pushed, you will have additional replaceable shields right on the ground, the use of which is much faster than the use of healing items.Due to the fact that the game has a high TTK, as well as there is a kill feed (kill statistics) in Apex, there is a small feature that is much less common in other games - suckers, or a third squad in a simpler language. This is both caniceer and the beauty of the game. Only someone knocks out, as everyone sees this information in the statistics of murders, and if you heard the shots of the weapon that is indicated in the kill feed, then this is the weakened squad of opponents that you can "finish". At high ranks, everything can turn into pure absurdity when no one fights, but as soon as someone knocks out, half of the server flies into the house with a weakened squad in the hope of finishing off the survivors.No matter how good the game is, there will definitely be a lot of problems in it. And if you can tell about the features of the game in a couple of lines, then you can talk about the problems for hours. To be honest, when creating the article, I initially wrote out all the problems that I want to sort out, and only then added positive points so as not to roll everything into a negative.If you go to the top of the twitch, you can find tier 1 esports players who regularly play on bronze and kill half of the server. Respawn, like EA, simply ignore them and do not ban them. Well, why? They bring traffic. Schoolchildren, seeing how the streamer kills everyone in a row, enter the game in the hope that they will also be able to, and then they themselves die from such streamers. From the outside, it looks like a wolf was released into a flock of sheep, but they don't care, although this directly affects the ranking mode.More recently, a scandal surfaced in which it turned out that some teams, mainly NA and EU, have their own analysts and data miners who pulled information about impossible zones from the game files, which gave them a huge advantage. After all, knowing that the zone cannot be in a certain place, they could have taken a more advantageous place in advanicee to win. There was no reaction from the developers, which is also funny, because such information is generally stored in the game client.Every season is tested before release, and Respawn gives access to the test client only to some professional players, which is not fair, for the reason that they know what will be in it, and at the end of the season they will already be prepared than other teams. The developers deliberately ignore some teams, give access mainly to NA teams, for example, 2 teams with CIS players Aurora and Fire Beavers do not have access to the test, despite the fact that they took 1st and 4th place at the recent ALGS 2022-23: Split 1 Pro League - EMEA (something like a qual on lan, but with prize money, specifically these quals were between EU+CIS).

text: Key: battle royale = Battle royale, TF = Titan Fall, TDM = Team Death MatchApex Legends, a game based in the universe of the amazing Titanfall universe is a Battle royale game featuring movement reminiscent of the hyper energetic movement found within the TF games although not as complicated and involved. The combat and firefights feel amazing and honestly in my opinion feature the best gun-play of any battle royale Game currently. The legend selection in the game offers multiple play-styles that fit almost any type of player. From your defensive tanky-esc characters to your assassin run and gun style ones there is something for you to pick no matter what you enjoy. Often there is events going on in-game such as the season battle-pass as well as seasonal events like Halloween, Winter, and other seasonal themes. Some events bring in fun game-modes which change up the game-play in minor or crazy ways. One of my favorites is the mode added during Halloween which when killed turns you into a zombie of sorts which you then continue playing in order to kill the survivors before they evac on a ship. Another mode that exists is the constant rotating playlist of games such as TDM, Control Points, Gun Game, and a few more. This mode offers some good pass time stuff to do when tired of dying constantly from bad teams in normabad games or just wanting to chill a bit. While there is a ranked play mode I have very little experienicee with it as I don't have a group I play with often at this time. From the experienicee I do have it is fun but can be a bit more of a "sweaty" group of players to play with as they often care a bit more about legend combos and such.The game price is Free to Play but your heavily eniceouraged to pay some money to buy every new skin. Obviously you do not HAVE to buy them but when you see players using the newer expensive skin, new legend that you likely don't have enough currenicey for sinicee you spent it on something recently, and the heirloom melee weapon you just NEED for your main legend.TLDR: Overall Apex Legends is a fun game pulling many weapons and movement skills from it is  developers other game TItanfull while adding in fun Battle royale combat and great characters to spice up the gameplay.

text: Ladies and gentlemen, gather 'round for a hilariously zany review of Apex Legends, the battle royale game where you and your squad drop into an arena and pray that your aim is better than your ability to choose landing spots.First things first, picking a landing spot in Apex Legends is like trying to find your keys in the dark while wearing oven mitts. You'll argue with your teammates about where to drop, and when you finally agree, you'll be greeted by a dozen other squads who had the same brilliant idea. it is  like a chaotic game of musical chairs with bullets.And speaking of teammates, Apex Legends is all about teamwork, or as I like to call it, "Teamwork: The Game Where You Hope Your Squadmates Don't Loot Everything Before You Even Land." You'll spend precious seconds pinging items and desperately hoping they'll share the loot, but they'll probably just run off with the good stuff, leaving you with an empty backpack and hurt feelings.Now, let's talk about the legends themselves. Each character has unique abilities that sound amazing on paper but often end up being about as useful as a chocolate teapot. You'll watch in horror as your ultimate ability misses every shot or your fanicey tactical move only succeeds in getting you killed. it is  like trying to perform magic tricks at a toddler's birthday party - the audienicee is unimpressed.But here's the kicker: Apex Legends is addictive like that bag of potato chips you can't stop eating. Even when you're getting your behind handed to you by a squad of teenage prodigies, you'll keep coming back for more. it is  like a dysfunicetional relationship you just can't quit.And let's not forget the ping system. it is  like communicating with a series of emojis and interpretive danicee moves. You'll ping an enemy, and your teammate will interpret it as "Let's have a tea party." But hey, at least it is  good for a laugh.Lastly, the character banter is like listening to a buniceh of superheroes argue about who gets the last slice of pizza. it is  a mix of hilarious one-liners and sassy comebacks that make you wish you were hanging out with these legends in real life.So, there you have it, Apex Legends, the game where choosing a landing spot is a life-or-death decision, teamwork is a crapshoot, and character abilities are like trying to jugood gamele flaming chainsaws. it is  a rollercoaster of chaos and hilarity, and you'll keep playing even when it drives you to the brink of insanity.

text: Most of my hours in this game are in Origin.Sadly I can't recommend it in current state, the core mechanics are iniceredibly solid, the movement, the gunplay, TTK, really well executed and thought out. But there are several issues that absolutely KILL the game:1. Aim assist is wayyy to strong and puts MnK players at huge disadvantage particularly at close range fights, this is my single bigood gameest issue with the game, playing against people that essentially have aimbots removes any fun you could potentially have. There is no option to turn off crossplay at all for PC players so your are stuck with lobbies full of console sweats that will track you automatically through smoke and one clip you with barely any input.2. MMR is completely broken, even if you are bronze you will be put up against diamond or higher in competitive, currently is serves absolutely no purpose and many players simply resort to ratting which is hiding until the end of the game, no fun at all.3. Cheaters, every lobby will pretty much have one cheater, this being a free game people are not afraid to be banned as they will simply spoof their hardware ID, make another free account and cheat again. I would support making a paid premium version of the game like CS GO where if you are banned you would lose 20-30 euros.Edit: Oh, and I forgot all the woke shit too, annoying but does not affect the gameplay

text: My Lightning-Fast Apex Adventure: 0.3 Hours of Pure Chaos!Well, folks, gather 'round, because I've got the ultimate review of Apex Legends after a whopping 0.3 hours of gameplay. You know, they say first impressions are everything, and let me tell you, Apex didn't waste any time impressing me!In the brief time I spent in the game, I managed to jump into a match, land somewhere, and find out that "respawn" isn't just a button you press in real life. Turns out, you actually need teammates for that! Who knew?The graphics? Oh, they were stunning for the nanoseconds I had to appreciate them. it is  like the game was teasing me with it is visual greatness, only to remind me that I'd better start running, or I'll be the next target practice for the pros.Speaking of running, it turns out I have a hidden talent for sprinting through the battlefield like my life depends on it, which, in Apex Legends, it probably does. Trust me, Usain Bolt would be jealous of my mad dash skills.The characters are like superheroes, and I was the clumsy sidekick trying to keep up. My poor choice of legend probably didn't help. I think I picked the one with the faniceiest hat, but alas, hats can't save you from the fierce competitors in this game.To sum it up, Apex Legends is like a roller coaster. it is  thrilling, intense, and over before you know it. If you're looking for a game to test your reflexes and your ability to adapt to a virtual battlefield in record time, Apex Legends might just be the game for you. Or maybe not. I'll let you know when I've played it for a full hour!
label: TRUE

Apex Legends has the potential to regain it is status as a beloved title, but it must navigate these challenges with care and player satisfaction at the forefront.

text: Notoriously bugood gamey and punishing, Apex Legends is one of the worst battle royale experienicees I've ever played (don't let the 2 hours on record fool you: I have 10 hours on Xbox). This could be due to design decisions or my skill level, but I'm leaning towards the former considering that I'm very good at online shooters but always seem to be matched up against sweats in Apex. Detailed breakdown follows:GRAPHICSVery cartoonish in comparison to the Titanfall franicehise on which this is based. The graphics aren't inherently bad, but they do look quite dated, which could be due to Respawn using the Source Engine which is as old as the hills. 6/10GAMEPLAYGuns are inaccurate, certain character abilities are overpowered which leads to certain operators becoming pay-to-win, lagood gamey servers leads to matches playing out terribly, and then don't even get me started on the amount of grind needed to get stuff without paying money (more on that later). Only fun aspect is traversal and looting. 4/10STORYApex eschews the campaigns of previous Titanfalbad games and adopts the GAAS trend of evolving narratives. Personally I've always found this narrative hard to follow and never interesting to invest time into. 5/10ONLINEThis is an online-only game that has no solos option. You're forced to matchmake with other people. When playing with friends this isn't an issue, but the majority of the community that I've experieniceed is very unwelcoming and not willing to show new players the ropes. Not to mention the fact that anti-cheat seems non-existent (coming from someone on PC) and there are a lot of cheaters that I've seen in my 12 hours total with the game. 4/10BUGS/OPTIMIZATIONEver wonder why Apex Legends patch notes are so long, even when it is a normal hotfix? it is  because the game engine is being held together by duct tape, screws, and unanswered prayers from Hell. The game is notoriously unoptimized, suffers from screen-tearing, lag issues, framerate drops, and hitches throughout the game. This is what happens when studios decide to make a game with an already outdated engine and then continue to update the game over 4 years later. 3/10DLC/MONETIZATIONDespite being a free game, Apex Legends is plagued by overmonetization. Let me explain: All new characters are locked behind a paywall. Most cosmetics are locked behind the battle pass. Apex runs multiple seasonal events called Collection Events that don't even give you free cosmetics, you have to pay to grind for those items. And onicee you've no-lifed the game enough to finish the event and grab the heirloom, you realize you've pretty much paid your power bill in Apex Coins. I'm not kidding, the game is so expensive to get everything it is  not even funny. 2/10VERDICTApex Legends should be avoided at all costs. Broken gameplay, poor online experienicee, and predatory monetization hold this game back from being worthy of the Titanfall name. I can't believe EA sacrificed Titanfall 3 for this. Actually I can believe it sinicee EA is the king of poor decisions. 4/10

text: PC players can not  disable crossplay. So you are stuck with open mic console players with aim assist turned on. This alone is enough to dismiss this game. I am still going to play it with some friends, but it deserves none of your money as a PC player unless this option is added back in.Beyond that I feel like this title lacks an identity or features that other titles don't do better. The ultimate abilities are obviously inspired by Overwatch. Some of them are exact copies, while others are modifications on their ideas. None of these abilities have the match defining impact that they do in Overwatch. They are often infrequently used or not used at all. I've gone entire matches without seeing any of them trigood gameered. To make matters worse (lol), many of the ultimate abilities are not locked to your team. So, for example a player can place a portal and both enemies and friendlies can utilize it. What logic is there in that?Next is tactical abilities. These are slightly more impactful, but still lack pop. I can fire off an entire swarm of rockets with a tactical ability only for my enemy to come out with maybe 25% HP loss. Less damage than a few bullets would have done. The guns are right out of any other FPS like COD. They are mostly generic and sometimes I think there are to many variations of the same weapon types. Everyone gravitates toward the same few meta guns. So picking up anything lesser just leaves you at a disadvantage. No hero here has truly unique weapons. Finally is the cheats. Part of which I blame on the developers. Sinicee they forced PC and Console players together. PC players have discovered how to get aim assist working with mouse and keyboard. In addition to this jittering aiming is highly prevalent in this title to cut down on recoil. Either manually (legit) or script/macro based (cheating). Either way, your aim matters less than your ability to control the hellish recoil on the guns in this game. Overwatch even in it is downgraded Overwatch 2 formula is still a better title than this for hijinks, good ultimates and fun weapons. Titles like COD are still better straight up run and gun shooters. Apex is lost somewhere in the middle. Unable to decide what it is and not doing a very good job copying these other titles.
label: FALSE

text: So, after about 25 hours in this game I could tell you something.First of all, game is easy to learn but hard to master, you should learn about movement, mechanics of legends(characters) and their skills. Also, you should master at least main weapons that people mostly use in this game.Good thing that this game does not have any toxic players, I've never met someone who said something bad to me. Also, players can tell you about how to do something if you're new player. Comparing to Warzone right now I like this game better, because I've also never experieniceed any bugs or cheaters. I really like it because I can just chill and play.Perfomanicee & graphics are pretty good, even some Potato PC can run this game, but mid-range PC I guess will be better. I running game all low, to have good vision and also have more then 144+ fps, but it is  still look pretty and my computer fell himself very chill and good.The best legends for me Bangalore, Bloodhound and Lifeline, most easiest to handle when you're newbie, so I can recommend to start with them. But all legends are good balaniceed, so you're not gonna have any problem with imbalaniceed legends.Battle Pass and other paid things well priced, you can buy Battle Pass for 9 dollars and easily grind max level. TDM mode can give you some more fun and you're also gonna get XP from it. I play this mode when I'm overwhelmed with Battle royale or very angry and tired.As a result, I recommend that every fan of the Battle royale genre or those who want to try at least take a look at Apex Legends. I think this game will be able to hold a lot of people for a long time.

text: So, here's the deal with Apex Legends – it is  like a crazy game of "let's pretend" but with guns, legends with cool abilities, and a ring that's way too interested in squishing us. You drop onto the map with your squad, and suddenly you're a superhero, but with more pings and fewer capes.First off, let's talk about the legends. These folks are like action figures on a sugar rush. You've got Wraith, who can disappear and reappear like she's playing hide and seek. Pathfinder? He's like your buddy's pet robot with a knack for grappling onto cliffs and slinging you into trouble. And don't even get me started on Mirage's holographic danicee parties.Now, when it comes to loot, it is  like a treasure hunt. You'll be shouting "dibs" on that sweet weapon skin or that fanicey helmet like it is  a game of Monopoly, and you just landed on Park Place. And let's not forget the Mozambique – the pistol that's so bad it is  become a running joke. But hey, sometimes you just have to embrace the Mozambique madness.As for teamwork, it is  a mixed bag. You'll have those moments where your squad moves like a well-oiled machine, and then there are times when someone ziplines into the enemy squad, screaming "Woo-hoo!" We've all been there.But here's the beauty of it all: even if you don't win, you're guaranteed a laugh. Whether it is  from the legends' witty banter or your friend's spectacular fail, Apex Legends is like a comedy show with bullets.So, if you're ready for a game that'll have you laughing, shouting "dibs," and occasionally pulling off some epic wins, give Apex Legends a shot. it is  not about the victory; it is  about the hilarious ride to get there!

text: STAY AWAY!New player experienicee, and it is  by far the worst gaming experienicee I've ever had the displeasure of living through (barely). This is a long one.First warning: This is NOT Grindr. The way it constantly invades your behind you could easily think it is, but on Grindr at least you have a chanicee of a kiss first.If you are a new player looking for a negood game, stay away. I repeat, don't even think of touching the load of crap that this game is right now. Apex is NOT interested in new players. STAY AWAY.There are two major issues that completely ruins the new player experienicee in this game. The first one with separate but no less horribly designed flaws that contribute to the larger issue.1.There is NO new player pathway to get to know the game in a fun and "easy" way. As a lvl 1 account you have access to 3 game modes which is a playlist of non Battle royale maps such as zone control, and then you have battle royale duo or trio but not ranked. To be able to join ranked your account needs to be lvl 50, which took me and my friend about 90 hours. So just to be able to join a playlist where decent matchmaking is expected you need to play through a single player campaign worth of time.So you're stuck with these 3 modes to grind for 90+ hours and it is a horrifying grind. The Apex matchmaking has created it is new category of completely useless. In our first games we were constantly matched against people with 1000's of kills, and when our KDA dropped close to absolute zero we instead "only" got people with 100's of kills. IF you're unlucky enough that the game accidentally gives you a match where you happen to win, then you're screwed. You're back at practically every match having diamond+ players on the other teams. Will you get matches without diamonds? Sure, but I can count those matches on one hand. The times I've wanted to send hate mails to the devs I've lost count of.But say you stay and try to hit that lvl 50 mark to get into ranked, because there surely it'll get better right? First you have 10 placement matches, in my first I had 10+ diamond trails, which kinda makes sense you get tested against the best to place. Same thing the rest of the 10 games. Great I got placed in wood 2 (below bronze) that has to mean I'll get matched against other suckers like me right? No. My friend and I were Rookie II, we had a Bronze III friend join (cause ranked is only trios) and here we go again. Diamond trails all over the place, top players having 1000's of kills. Bad first luck? I've played 5 more ranked games and it hasn't been any different. it is  like Apex says they've implemented a ranked system but forgot to add the actual code for placing you in a rank or adding a rank limit for any matches. I can go on for ages about the Abyssmal matchmaking Apex does not have but I think I've made my point clear.------------------------------------2.The second major issue is cross platform, which is a great thing when you want it. But as a PC player YOU CAN'T TURN THAT OFF. Consoles can, PC can't. And that shouldn't be too much of an issue right you have a mouse that moves way more precise than sticks, only what Apex did was to give controllers a ******* gluestick of Aim-assist. It does not matter if you have 100 more hours than the controller you're up against if you're in a 1v1 he will down you majority of the times because he can't miss. When most of the streamers of a game switches to playing on console it is  a very telling sign that you have an issue, this has been voiced by the community but Apex does not care.3.And that's the third category of issues with Apex that aren't game breaking in the same way. They don't care about you. I had several technical issues in the beginning and I posted several threads on different forums iniceluding Apex own technical forum. On Apex forum I never got a reply, on the other threads the answers ranged from "gitgood" or "yeah Apex sucks you just have to deal with it". The Apex support team is nonexistent except if you post in an unsupported language, and the Apex player community is with a FEW exceptions just straight out toxic.------------------------------------And it makes me sad. Because the game is fun! The champs are unique with lots of fun ideas, the weapons and items are good and allow you to form your own play style. The maps are good and gives you great opportunities to utilize the unique play styles the champs have. But these positives are nothing compared to the horrible matchmaking, the unfair device configurations, the heap of technical issues, and the community. Sure it is  free, but in it is current state you should get paid to play it. I would've had a better time counting grains of sand in a desert for 90hrs.I can't stress enough how bad of an overall experienicee Apex has been. There aren't enough numbers below 0 for me to rate this game. DON'T TOUCH.

text: That a game with so much money behind it can be so consistently horrible is astounding. I've played it sinicee the beginning and the fact that the same audio issues from season one have only gotten worse is truly impressive. Why do custom cross-hairs on hip fire not exist? Who knows. Why are Horizons footsteps completely silent? Who knows. Why are the servers so bad? Who knows. For a AAA game the ball is dropped so consistently with this game that I can only hope both it and EA are erased from gaming history. Apex had a lot of potential when it first came out, and a lot of the time it looks like they're changing things for the better. The problem is that for every good thing they do they have a tendenicey to bring along 2 bad things with it. The fact that "The Core" has replaced the mirage ship map is a tragedy. Team deathmatch does not work on "The Core" at all. The map is far too open, and lacks any real variety. Most of your time will be spent rushing the center of the map every match, and who could've guessed, but having a 6 vs 6 in that type of environment sucks. It does not feel as bad in gun run, but it is still not designed for it. They literally just ripped a point from broken moon and called it a map. Didn't even bother to do the bare minimum by adding cover so you wouldn't be tunneled into fighting in the 4 uphill corridors. Mixtape was the one saving grace that Apex has had for a while, but it is  been completely ruined by either the dev's laziness or upper managements iniceompetenicee. I'd like to think that they have someone capable of making good tdm maps given that 2 of the arena maps did pretty well while they were still in rotations, but they clearly haven't been listening to them if they do. tldr: The devs typically ignore core issues with the game. Mixtape which was the only fun mode, is filled with frustrating maps. Features that you would expect in any modern fps, such as crosshair adjustment, are completely missing.

text: The hidden MMR in Apex Legends is honestly messed up. Players should be ranked in ranked games based on their skills, like KD ratios and how many matches they win. That's the whole point – you get better, your rank goes up, and you play against better players. It should be straightforward and clear so everyone knows where they stand. But Apex throws this whole system out the window with their hidden MMR. It secretly rates your skill level and matches you with players of similar skills, but it does not show you this rating. So, even though you're playing against more challenging opponents like you would if you ranked up, your visible rank does not change, and that isn't very clear. It feels like your actual in-game rank does not mean anything, and it takes away from the satisfaction of climbing the ranks and getting better at the game. It does not make sense to have a ranking system if Apex uses hidden MMR to match players.

text: There is something different about this Battle royale FPS game compared to the others. Even though the loot system can be even more complex than CoDMW2, it is so much easier to actually use. Put a little time into the game and you will soon have a couple of nice skins with nothing needed to spend on them. There are really cool futuristic features like zip lines all over and jetpack redeploying to avoid the arena closing. I ended up doing a random trio for my first game and guess what? We won. First game. That just does not happen on games like Fortnite, MW2, or Valorant. Sure I got carried a little bit but I didn't die and had a positive KD. And I even got a second win by the end of that first session. Whatever it is about this game, whether it be the more realistic graphics or more customizable characters, I really like it as my next battle royale shooter.

text: There's a lot of criticisms that I can lay down on the game right now. But the urgent one that I think is underrated is the seemingly inicereasing false bans towards people playing this game on Linux distros with no proper way to follow-up with customer support, and is what drove me to unfortunately review Apex negatively after all this time.Why does this matter despite Linux's playerbase likely being low? Well, this game is veryfied to be Steam Deck playable, and Valve's device just so happens to run an Arch Linux-based distro. So even with differenicees across Linux distros, if the false bans are due to an implied blanket ban for non-Windows users, that's a slap in the face to being veryfied by Valve/Steam. Losing customers is the last thing I think EA/Respawn would want sinicee they care about money so much, so I can only hope they undo those false bans as based on the reports I've seen, nothing related to specific software or cheating has been found to be the cause of these Linux-based bans -- just the sheer fact of using Linux alone.Why does this matter to me personally? Well, sinicee Season 14 I rotate between Windows and Linux because I tend to prefer using different operating systems depending on audio or video hobby editing or whatnot, and Apex is one of the notable big games that works consistently fine across Linux-based distros for the most part -- from Manjaro to Pop! OS to ZorinOS to EndeavourOS, the fact that I can jump into MP matches regardless of the OS I use, amidst all of the issues the game has, is something that still eniceourages me to play. And as much as I regret paying some money to this game, I did technically do that for some packs and battle passes in the hopes of getting some cool skins.While I thankfully have never been banned playing this game, if these mistaken bans keep on happening, you are driving more paying customers away from you, moreso than the shitty decision-making that has been done on this game over the years. We want at the very least acknowledgment of the false bans, and re-evaluating ensuring accounts that used Linux to game only are reopened -- it is  not like EA/Respawn is doing a good job in banning actual cheaters anyway, as I still see streamers face them from time to time to this day, AND DDOSed servers are still a thing to this day.That's all for now.

text: This game is awful, let me explain........The graphics suck, it looks like I'm playing a 20 your old game. The servers are lagood gameing a few days a week, I have 300 mbps by the way. There's "rats" ruining other players' games. Predators using smurf accounts. The game does not give you fair fights based on skills and unfortunately the game is slow for most of the time until you get into the last few circles. But wait, it gets worse. So first of all you get online and want to get into a ranked game but spend forever waiting for the game to start. On the lower ranks the game might be able to find a session quicker but after you climb the ranked ladder you can expect to be sitting there for 20 minutes, maybe even 30 minutes to get into a game. You can play non ranked games to find sessions quicker but you can expect others to troll, lag out, or ruin your game by doing their own thing sinicee they can leave at any moment without penalty. So I don't recommend non ranked games. When you finally get into a game. There's a good chanicee the only character you're good at gets taken. So it means you won't be playing well during that game. Then you either land where other teams are landing and have a high chanicee of dying, or you land where no one landed and play a looting simulator. Keep in mind if you die at the beginning you're going right back at the menu waiting another 30 minutes for a game to start. So you better do your best to stay alive. But unfortunately you can't really survive an attack without all of your teammates working together. This game at it is core is a team game. All it takes to ruin your game is one of your teammates leaving, ratting, or playing alone. Ratting is the term used for the players who don't actually play the game, but instead decide to hide during the entire game in one spot for placement. So if someone is missing in the team during a fight for whatever reason. If the opponent has three players working together but have worse aim you'll probably still lose. In this game you really can't 1v3 or 2v3 often because the time to kill someone is higher than other games. But lets imagine you won a 1v3, you better keep aware of your surroundings. Even though it is  not fair your first teammate left and your second teammate lagood gameed out. You fought a full team alone and still won. But you gotta remember this is apex you're playing here, and now there's a full stacked predator team who's perfect at jumping into a fight right as they end. Pulling off what we call a third party. You unfortunately die because even though you would've won a fair 1v1 with each one of them. They played the game the way it was attended, they worked together, and timed their fights correctly. If you want the best experienicee with this game, don't match up with randoms. The randoms are toxic in this game. Most sound like Imperial Hal in a 100k tournament screaming at his team for being a piece of $#!+ player. Even though a lot of the time. They're the one rushing into every single fight they can without caring if their team is with them. Then they die and get angry because you didn't help. But how are you supposed to keep up when they're moving quicker because they're playing a mobility character. The game is so stressful. Don't play this to relax ever. The only reason you should play this game is if you're really into challenging, and competitive games. There's a lot of mobility tech to learn. A lot of character abilities to learn. Gun recoil patterns to learn. Diversity in play styles. Unique maps. Questionable developer decisions. Which every now and then makes certain characters better than others for a while until it is  fixed. With this game you need to grind it 24/7 to keep up with others because new tech and meta play styles are being discovered all the time. If you're not learning any of it. What we need is not more maps or legends or skins. What we need is for the maps and legends and mechanics that already exist to be reworked and improved. In it is current state many characters and weapons are bugood gamey, overpowered, and underpowered. Many maps have glaring issues and poor designs which make for long drawn out matches in which players must either hot drop into a massive fighting pit or spend the majority of the match wandering the map and eniceountering one or two teams before dying or winning the match. Many players experienicee frequent issues with audio, server quality, cheating, exploits, and more. Despite all these issues, what we see most from the devs are new characters, maps and weapons which serve to add more bugs and frustration to the game, as well as worsen the already terrible game balanicee. New legends have begun to serve similar funicetions to already existing characters such as Ash and Wraith or Seer and Bloodhound. New skins often are now recolors or variants of older designs. New maps focus more on expanding the play area and bringing in new features rather than improving the core gameplay elements and honing in on the fundamentals. The devs also seem generally slow to change and out of touch with the playerbase. I've seen complains and requests go years before being addressed. I recall the long wait before crossplay was even considered before being announiceed and introduced. Now players complain about the poor implementation of crossplay and how it is exploited by parts of the playerbase. Having played sinicee release on PS4 and for a while now on PC, the game feels radically worse than when I started. I would much rather play the launiceh version of Apex compared to it is current state. The game would benefit from less legends, less maps, less weapons, and less gimmics. text: This game is pretty good, I kind of wish we got Titanfall 3 instead but i'll take this as a replacement. Even though you're not running on walls and dropping mechs it still has a very similar game feel. Lots of unique guns with very specific mechanics alongside a buniceh of characters that are all interesting in their own unique way. Very good focus on mobility with a fun sliding system and the best ping implementation of any game i've ever played. Writing this at the time of the revenant rework season.I play this game off and on: started when the game launicehed, played until season 2, quit until rampart came out, played until season 8 (thank god it moved to steam at this point!), quit until season 11, played until season 12, and now i'm back at season 18. I bring up this specific pattern because I think this "play it a lot for a few seasons then quit for a while" pattern is the preferred way to enjoy Apex; I think I would go insane if I kept playing this game hardcore without taking many long breaks, and you kind of have to dedicate yourself to a season (more on that later). Lots of people are complaining about MMR both in casuals and ranked, and I think if you are that committed to playing this game optimally you probably won't have much fun with it. Apex feels like the most "party game" battle royale i've played in the sense that lots of stupid crap happens regularly and is actively eniceouraged by both map and character design, so I personally believe if you think too hard about your matchmaking rating or rank you're likely just going to be miserable. I play ranked sometimes until I get to gold or platinum (onicee I got diamond, that was exciting), which is when the loss RP gets so high that it is  no longer fun to bother with. This is all to say I think you should probably go into this game thinking about it like a silly arcade shooter with good gunplay and fun movement mechanics, rather than a high-end esport.Sinicee it is  a F2P game and those are becoming the scourge of everyone's time and money these days, I should probably spend a little bit to talk about monetization. To me Apex has always felt generous on your wallet but harsh on the time commitment needed, but I could see how other people would potentially disagree. You [i]can[/i] get the battle passes for completely free forever as you get premium currenicey from each battle pass. You get a very little amount for free but when you upgrade the pass you get enough premium currenicey to buy the next one (plus a little bit extra). The problem then is that this is one of the slowest battle passes to complete out of the F2P games i've played... I can imagine there's worse, but to get to 100 each season you're going to have to sit down and grind out almost every challenge and a buniceh of extra games on top of that. It will take even longer if you're not very good at the game and/or deal low damage each match, you're iniceentivized to hot drop and shoot as many people as possible vs playing games for a long time. Because of this I only purchase the premium battle pass if i'm at rank 50 or above for the season and still feel like playing, so I don't potentially waste the F2P currenicey i've slowly accumulated. Speaking of F2P currenicey, the free currenicey you get by playing the game that allows you to unlock legends accumulates very slowly. It does not feel so bad to me sinicee I'm level ~175 so I have a lot of legends unlocked, but sinicee there's no free legend rotation as far as i'm aware I could see playing the stock guys very repetitive after a while. The levelling system is also kind of weird as you actually get less rewards the higher you level, which feels like it is  punishing dedicated players and forcing them to pay more money... but I guess I can see how this would be a successful business strategy sinicee those are the players more likely to become whales.I haven't really talked much in depth about the actuabad gameplay in this review because it does not really matter for F2P games. If it at all interests you, check it out and play it- it is  free. it is  these weird things orbiting the game that impact the experienicee more, and what I think new players are looking to understand. The TL;DR of the "weird f2p elements" then would be: I think the monetization is "fine" and more time consuming than wallet breaking (unless you want heirlooms then god help you), the live service bit is are less annoying than other battle royales i've played as not [i]as[/i] much changes from season to season, MMR being iniceluded in casuals as well is unfortunate but should frankly just be ignored. If you want to try out a fun movement based/character based shooter then check it out, then go ahead and start feeling bad about how Respawn is basically shackled to this game until it stops making money and we'll never get a new Titanfall.

The bigood gameest example of this is the superglide: by pressing a certain combination of keys at the perfect frame, after climbing something, you can have an insanely big speed boost. The issue with this is that it is frame dependant, which means that, the higher FPS, the lower your chanicee is of hitting the buttons in the right frame. The game has all of these "beautiful" movement techs, but at the same time slows you down when you get shot. What the fuck is the point of that? Audio is totally broken, you will NEVER hear someone come up right behind you, and it is  insane that in all this time it has never been fixed. The other very not fun part of the game is [strike] aimbot[/strike] aim assist for controller. It is so strong that some pros literally play controller in pro tournaments, and if this isn't just bonkers in an FPS game I don't know what is.If you play kb&m it does not matter how long you've practiced and if your aim is good, you WILL get shit on by 9 year old jimmys playing on their xbox, because aim assist is that fucking strong.This has been an issue sinicee the start of the game and has been getting worse and worse as people found even more ways to abuse aim assist, such as using rewasd or using XIM. The main advantage that kb&m has had over controller, which is movement, has also been circumvented thanks to configs, and that means that controller really is just the winning option here. In an FPS game. EA has of course done fuck all to address this issue, and god forbid they nerf the aim assist, they need to make kids feel like they're good at the game so they get hooked and spend more in skins.

text: Wanted to wait until 1000 hours to write anything, though admittedly I knew for the last ~500 where this was going to end up. I LOVE this game at it is core. The actuabad game loop, the gamePLAY - wonderful stuff. Would literally never get old IF the matchmaking were...different(?) Stop me if you've heard this one before... To preface this, I want to address the point delusional EA worshipers constantly throw back at my particular critique, before anyone even says it this time: Not everyone has the will, let alone desire, to browse Reddit forums in an effort to find similarly skilled randoms to play with in order to simulate a balaniceed matchmaking system. People on the internet are weird + I'm not a social butterfly. Sifting through all that shit is not for me and I don't think that's a reasonable expectation to have of your playerbase. What I WANT to do is play with my actual friends, who have historically been willing, to their credit, to endure many sessions with me. Those sessions typically involve(d) them being instantly one-clipped to start an engagement by players they NEVER should've been facing in pubs, let alone ranked, and leaving me to attempt to 1v2/3, again, MOST of the time. I'm historically a 2-ish KD player at my best; I'm solid at the game, but I'm simply not capable of consistently beating Master and Predator-tier 3-stacks who populate our lobbies, especially not in a numbers disadvantage. But that's how it goes. Matchmaking sees my relatively funicetional skill level, ignores my friends' substantially lower skill potential, and away we go for 12 straight crushing defeats before someone decides this is a waste of time. And I can't fault them! It is absolutely a waste of time to just get steamrolled over and over and over because the game used your twitching corpse to fill ultra-high MMR lobbies instead of offering any kind of balaniceed experienicee. And imagine how much worse it is for my friends who are already weak players, trying to come back after extended breaks from the game. Forget about it. I've been on hiatus from Apex for the better part of a calendar year now, but every onicee in a while I get the urge to jump back in, thinking SURELY this time it'll be different. I read the other day a post from EA/Respawn (whatever) from the beginning of 2022 describing the "previous" matchmaking system in detail (which I never expected them to ever do) and going pretty vaguely through their alleged plans to rework and revamp said system. They even admitted that the longstanding format, as everyone suspected, was simply matching the entire party to the best player's skill level for the purpose of finding an "appropriate" lobby. But supposedly their preliminary testing data for this new approach supported a more *gasp* balaniceed matchmaking experienicee for the majority of skill levels, and they intended to phase out the "highest skill" matchmaking that had made the game so caniceerous for so long. I figured, shit, this was almost a year ago! Surely by NOW it is  fully implemented and I can actually rally the lads onicee more for the ultimate revenge round! Finally I can queniceh my thirst for this awesome but unplayable game! Then I browse the Steam discussions and some Reddit threads. By all accounts the matchmaking is worse than ever before, with all evidenicee pointing to some unannouniceed matchmaking change taking place over the summer. This is the overwhelming consensus online, even factoring out the people who are obviously just irrationally mad or calling everyone a cheater. I firmly believe based on my thousand hours in this game that the cheaters, while they totally do exist, are almost all hanging out in high-level ranked games. it is  not common to find cheaters in pubs. However, I can totally understand how newer/less competent players would automatically assume they're facing cheaters when they're getting insta-beamed by players who should NEVER HAVE BEEN IN THEIR LOBBIES. it is  really, really sad to waste a game like this because of something so seemingly fixable. At least from a layman's perspective, why not just matchmake based on the AVERAGE skill level of your team? In our case, that would, in theory, largely squeeze out the top-1% players while also allowing in some people closer to my friends' level. They'd still have a challenge, but it'd be so much fairer for them. I would have a mixed experienicee, but much better chanicee of carrying a fight against a slightly stronger team if necessary. Let me be clear in that I do not by any means expect to be able to like, pub-stomp the way Preds do for views or some shit. I want a challenge that my friends can REASONABLY hold up against, at least SOME of the time. And don't give me that goofy BS about the playerbase being too small to support the kind of solution I'm describing. There's over 120,000 people playing on Steam as I write this on a random Tuesday. It just sucks. This is far and away my favorite PvP game of all time. I would play it daily if I could, even working full time. I would find time for this game. I would even spend money on skins and shit, knowing full well that EA is evil and does not deserve a penny from me. The game is that freakin' good. In theory. But I gotta stop lying to myself every few months just to be immediately disappointed each time I try to go back. When I mention Apex at this point my friends just laugh and then we return to reality. This game is unplayable. Each season has brought with it some kinda new bugs to exploit or server instability or poor map rotation, etc. But the unfair matchmaking has been the one glaring constant for seemingly the entirety of Apex's existenicee outside of maybe the beta run, during which I was too bad at the game to notice any critical nuanicees. But yeah, the community feedback loudly confirms this. Glaniceing up, I see the recent reviews on Steam have been crap. Shocking. Yet here we are, again, as always. Perhaps EA's market research sugood gameests that they shouldn't fix this problem for some disgusting micro-transaction-related reason. Honestly, at this point, I HOPE the reason is something outwardly malicious like that, if only to confirm that they have a vested interest in NOT fixing anything. Because otherwise there's just truly no excuse. RIP this game.

text: Well, after 1,000 hours of playing this game, I think I can fairly say that Apex is a great FPS game. The movement is personally my favorite aspect of this game, as the skill ceiling/expression is literally infinite. Plus, the gunplay is really fun. All of the guns are unique and at the time of review, I'd say the game/guns are pretty much the most balaniceed they've ever been. All of the legends are unique and offer different playstyles (i.e. focusing on defense, movement, recon, etc.). One of the only complaints I have is the lack of real events that add new LTM's and gamemodes. I really appreciated the addition of Mixtape, a playlist of 3 gamemodes aside from BR, but collection events are usually all that ever happen along with some battle royale event that's usually meh. Overall though, I personally believe Apex is one of the best battle royale and FPS games on the market currently, plus it is free. However, I can't leave a review for Apex without talking about the monetization. About $120 or so for an heirloom is ridiculous, and skins can be pretty expensive (I have spent money on this game btw). Still, it is  not P2W after all sinicee they're just skins, so it does not really matter honestly. All in all, I would give this game a 9.5/10, as it isn't perfect, but the sheer fun and potential this game has pretty much makes up for it is  flaws imo.

text: While the market is full of free-to-play shooters, does Apex stand out as worth your time?Respawn has made a name for itself by creating iniceredible multiplayer experienicees, and Apex Legends is the shining jewel in it is crown. Paired with lovable characters and iniceredible gunplay, it has quickly become one of the most popular Hero Shooters and Battle royales there is. In the years sinicee it is release, the game has only grown in terms of content and popularity, but that can consequently make it a little harder to get into. Here, we'll break down what you can expect from Apex Legends and if it is  right for you.What Apex Legends has over many shooters is the personality of it is heroes mixed with a constantly evolving world. This creates a sense of community around it, extending from the largest esports tournaments to more local competitions.A shooter needs more than just compelling, evolving characters; it requires gameplay to match them. Apex Legends is much more fast-paced than other battle royales, and each hero's unique abilities help it stand out against it is rivals. This is coupled with many maps that eniceourage frequent speedy skirmishes over drawn-out potshots or camping in a single spot. It plays more like a long-form deathmatch than your more traditional battle royale.As a multiplayer game, Apex Legends has the benefit of simply playing for however long you want, whenever you want. The main battle royale mode takes a small time investment, averaging around 15-20 minutes. There's no strict time limit on matches though they'll be forced to end by the time the ring fully closes, typically within 30 minutes.Presently, Team Deathmatch is the only other permanent game mode, though these matches take place on significantly smaller maps over a single round, ending onicee a team hit is 50 kills. This will usually take around 15-20 minutes on average as well, though matches will automatically end after 30 minutes.Core to a hero shooter is it is Heroes, and those require a bit more investment to unlock. Though you could spend 750 Apex Coins– the premium currenicey to purchase them directly– you also gain 600 Legend Tokens with every level up. Purchasing a Legend this way requires 12,000 Legend Tokens. In other words, you can afford a new Legend every 20 level-ups, a feat requiring fairly consistent play versus directly purchasing them.Another part of the game that would require significan not time is the Battle Pass. It comes with both a premium and free track, which both level up simultaneously. To level up the Battle Pass, you must complete daily and weekly challenges across multiple game modes.With the sporadic play of a few hours a week, you could decently reach the middle of the battle pass, though it would take completing every weekly challenge consistently to reasonably fill out the battle pass before it expires.Apex Legends is a Free-To-Play experienicee, so the upfront cost to play is non-existent. It does, however, come with multiple forms of monetization, some of which contain cosmetics that can only be acquired with Apex Coins.Apex Coins can be purchased in bundles ranging from 1,000 for $10, with the most expensive granting 11,500 for $100. Very few purchases will fall evenly within these numbers, likely leaving you with an odd amount of Apex Coins left over to eniceourage you to purchase more. Directly purchasing a new Legend costs 750 Apex Coins, leaving you with 250 Apex Coins that can't afford anything but Apex Packs.Apex Legends has a Battle Pass for each season, with a premium and free track. The premium tracks cost 950 Apex Coins. Also relevant is that the Battle Pass funicetionally pays itself back if you fully complete it, offering 1200 Apex Coins.This means that if you play the game enough to consistently complete the Battle Pass and the additional 25 levels it provides, it is  almost profitable to purchase it.The store also contains various other cosmetics and loot boxes. Loot boxes, known as Apex Packs, cost 100 Apex Coins, though the contents are almost entirely random. Other cosmetics can range from as low as 500 Apex Coins for a single weapon skin to as high as 5,000 Apex Coins for event cosmetics. The average bundle typically costs between 1,000-2,000 Apex Coins.Apex Legends is a genuinely fun battle royale that greatly benefit is from being free. There's a good selection of starter Legends to try out, and the battle pass does not feel like a requirement to get the full experienicee.That said, the learning curve can be daunting and lobbies will throw you in with more experieniceed players right away. Matchmaking is wonky, so you'll really have to tough it out if you want to get far. Bring a couple friends and make it a more enjoyable ride.

text: With a good amount of time put in the game and not being someone who spends 16 hours daily playing or 10 hours in Aim Lab everyday, I'm your average dude who plays the game for fun. In a previous life where I had time to train 4 hours every day, my aim was great and I knew every map like the back of my hand, making me stay on top of the game. But now as a more busy guy and with time having passed, I can give you a good description of the pros and cons. First of all, the new weapons are enjoyable. I am talking weapons as new as Nemesis to weapons like Car and Volt. The game is not very good at balaniceing these weapons. 17 rounds in the R-99? Do you even want players using that? The new characters are cool too, especially the charismatic, high-class Ballistic. The game nerfed literally every character that I played during a certain time and now with the new Revenant and some optimizations, I have some solid choices to play. Now let's stick to the horrible part of the game; the matchmaking. First of all, it sometimes takes a lot of time for sufficient amount of players to be found; and this is not a good sign. Second of all, you perform just a tiny bit well in one game, maybe do 1K damage or get a few kills and you are gonna face IMMORTAL GODS the next match. it is  even worse in ranked which is typically supposed to have a well-thought matchmaking system. You don't even need to perform well, you will just face sweaty, no-lifers who are double stacked and will just vaporize you; and let me add, this happens from Rookie to Diamond, all the way through. Maybe in the previous life where I trained hours after hours in the Range or in Aim Lab, I could be happy but I'm not. What is the point of a game with no matchmaking that will just get you infuriated? Thanks for reading. Hope this helps!
